questions.solution: $\mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is an acidic oxide, acting as an acidic flux.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ethyl iodide when heated with sodium in dry ether gives pure
questions.solution:
It is Wurtz reaction. $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{I}+2 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{IC}_2 \mathrm{H}_5-\xrightarrow[\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5-\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5+2 \mathrm{NaI}]{\text { Dry ether }}$
So, $\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_{10}$ is formed.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ reactions are favoured by
options: bulky groups on the carbon atom attached to the halogen atom
small groups on carbon atom attached to the halogen atom
questions.solution: Bulky groups on the carbon atom attached to the halogen atom will hinder attack by $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ mechanism due to steric hindrance and hence, would favour $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism.

questions.solution: Polar aprotic solvents such as DMSO increase the rate of $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ reactions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following is most reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reaction?
questions.solution:
Stability of corresponding carbocation
$\mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}-\stackrel{+}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}_2\gt\mathrm{CH}_2=\stackrel{+}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}\gt\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5^{+}$
So, $\mathrm{ClCH}_2-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}_2$ is most reactive towards nucleophilic substitution reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following compounds will not react with Mg metal in the presence of ether to give a Grignard reagent?
questions.solution: Compounds containing acidic hydrogen cannot be used in Grignard's synthesis and the compound $\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OHCH}_2 \mathrm{Br}$ contains acidic hydrogen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the aromatic compounds react fastest with methoxide ion?
questions.solution: A nitro from para positions activate more for $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} \mathrm{Ar}$ reaction due to electron withdrawing resonance effect than two nitro from meta positions by inductive effect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which halide will be least reactive in respect to hydrolysis?
questions.solution: Vinyl chloride is least reactive towards hydrolysis because of non-reactivity of chlorine atom due to resonance stabilization.

questions.q: Iso-propyl chloride undergoes hydrolysis by
questions.solution: Iso-propyl chloride is a $2^{\circ}$ halide and $2^{\circ}$ halides can undergo hydrolysis either by $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ or $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 2$ mechanism depending upon the nature of solvent used.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A set of compound in which the reactivity of halogen atom in the ascending order is:
questions.solution:
Stability of corresponding carbocation
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2\gt\mathrm{CH}_2=\stackrel{+}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{H}\gt\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5^{+}$. So, the correct order of reactivity of halogen atom is chlorobenzene, vinyl chloride, chloroethane.

questions.solution:
Wurtz reaction involves the reduction of alkyl halide with Na in ether.
$R X+2 \mathrm{Na}+X R \xrightarrow{\text { Dry ether }} R-R+2 \mathrm{NaX}$

questions.solution: Primary alkyl halides undergo bimolecular nucleophilic substitution most readily.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.solution:
The function of $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ in Friedel-Craft's reaction is to produce electrophile.
$R-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{AlCl}_3 \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Electrophile }}{R^{+}}+\mathrm{AlCl}_4^{-}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The following compound which cannot be prepared by Wurtz reaction?
questions.solution: Compounds containing two or more carbon atoms are prepared by Wurtz reaction. Thus, methane $\left(\mathrm{CH}_4\right)$ cannot be prepared by Wurtz reaction.

The product $A$ is :

For the reaction $\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}_2+\mathrm{HOCl} \longrightarrow A$
This is addition reaction, this reaction involves in unsaturated compound or that compound which has multiple bonds ( $=$ or $\equiv$ bonds)
According to Markownfikoff's rule, the -ve part of reagent is added to that carbon atom of the double bond which bears the least number of hydrogen atom.

questions.solution: Anti-Markownikoff's rule addition reaction is not observed in case of but-2-ene. The reason is that symmetrical alkenes do not exhibit anti-Markownikoff's addition.

questions.solution: Reaction of alkyne with HBr supports it addition nature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following alkenes will react fastest with $\mathrm{H}_2$ under catalytic hydrogenation conditions?
questions.solution: During catalytic hydrogenation, the hydrogens are transferred from the catalyst to the same side of the double bond. Evidently, smaller the number of $R$ substituents, lesser is the steric hinderance and hence faster is the rate of hydrogenation. Thus, option (a) with two $R$ groups on the same side of the molecule is correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Angle strain in cyclopropane is
questions.solution:
Angle strain, $\alpha=\frac{1}{2}\left[10 y^{\circ} 28^{\prime}-\theta\right]$
In case of cyclopropane,$\theta=60^{\circ}$
$\therefore \alpha=\frac{1}{2}\left(109^{\circ} 28^{\prime}-60^{\circ}\right)$
$=24^{\circ} 44^{\prime}$

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Which reagents would you use to carry out the reaction
Ethyl benzene $\longrightarrow 2$ and 4 - chloro 1 - ethyl benzene?
questions.solution: Ethyl in o/p directing group. Direct chlorination of ethyl benzene with $\mathrm{Cl}_2 / \mathrm{FeCl}_3$ would give mixture of 2 and 4-chloroethyl benzene.

questions.q: By Wurtz reaction, a mixture of methyl iodide and ethyl iodide gives
questions.solution:
Wurtz reaction
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{I}+2 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{IC}_2 \mathrm{H}_5 \xrightarrow[-2 \mathrm{NaI}]{\text { Dry ether }} \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_6+\mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{H}_8+\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_{10}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The low density of ice compared to water is due to :
questions.solution: In ice each O-atom is bonded with 4 H -atoms in a tetrahedral fashion. Two bonds are normal covalent bonds, while two are H-bonds (weaker and larger in length) and hence give rise to loose cage structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ice floats on water because:
options: its density is less than that of water
crystal structure of ice has empty space
questions.solution: Ice floats on water because its density is less than that of water which in turn is due to empty space in its crystal structure. Crystal structure of ice is regular hexagon with empty space at the centre.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is false about $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ ?
options: Act as both oxidising and reducing agent
Two OH bond lie in the same plane
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ is a pale blue liquid, it can be oxidised by ozone. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ acts as both oxidising and reducing agent. The value of dipole moment of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ is 2.1 D . Which suggest it cannot be planar infact it has open book like structure.
Two $\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{H}$ lie in different plane

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is not correct for $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}$ ?
options: Solubility of NaCl in it is more than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
questions.solution: The b.p. and viscosity of $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is higher than water. Solubility of NaCl in it is less than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ because reaction velocity is slightly less in $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}$ due to the that deuterium bond is strong than protium bond.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is responsible for hardness of water?
questions.solution: Temporary hardness of water is due to the presence of bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium while permanent hardness is due to the presence of soluble chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following option best describes the 'hydrogen economy?.'
options: Using dihydrogen in an efficient manner
Dihydrogen is carried and stored in the form of liquid or gas
Addition of dihydrogen to the reactant
questions.solution: It is a method of using dihydrogen in an efficient manner and carried or stored in the form of liquid or gas. dihydrogen releases more energy than petrol and in more eco-friendly and also can be used in fuel cells.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The solubility $\mathrm{CaF}_2$ is $s$ moles/litre. Then solubility product is
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}\mathrm{CaF}_{\mathrm{s}} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{~F}^{-} \\ {\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]=s} \\ {\left[\mathrm{~F}^{-}\right]=2 s} \\ K_{\text {sp }}=\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right] \cdot\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2=s \cdot(2 s)^2 \\ K_{\text {sp }}=4 s^3\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In qualitative analysis, $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ is added before $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$
questions.solution: In qualitative analysis, $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ is added before $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ to , decrease $\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]$concentration. $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ suppresses the ionisation of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ due to common ion effect. Otherwise basic radicals of group V and group VI will be precipitated along with Illrd group basic radicals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Find the pH value of the mixture containing 50 cc M HCl and 30 cc M NaOH solution, assuming both to be completely ionised.
questions.solution:
Total volume after mixing $=50+30=80 \mathrm{cc}$ 
Moles of HCl after mixing $=\frac{50}{80} \mathrm{M}$ 
Moles of NaOH after mixing $=\frac{30}{80} \mathrm{M}$
Remaining number of moles of HCl after mixing
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{50}{80}-\frac{30}{80}=0.25 \mathrm{M} \\
{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right] } & =0.25=2.5 \times 10^{-1} \\
\mathrm{pH} & =-\log \left[2.5 \times 10^{-1}\right] \\
& =1-0.3979=0.6021
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Degree of dissociation of an acid HCl is $95 \%$. 0.192 g of the acid is present in 0.5 L of solution. The pH of the solution is
questions.solution:
Number of moles of \(\mathrm{HCl}=\frac{0.192}{36.5}=0.0053\)
Volume \(=0.5 \mathrm{~L}\)
Molarity of \(\mathrm{HCl}=\frac{0.0053}{0.5}=1.06 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M}\)
\(\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]\)in HCl solution \(=1.06 \times 10^{-2} \times \frac{95}{100} \approx 1 \times 10^{-2}\)
\(\mathrm{pH}=-\log \left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=-\log \left(1 \times 10^{-2}\right)=2\)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The $\mathrm{pK}_a$ of acetic acid is 4.74. The concentration of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}$ is 0.01 M . The pH of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}$ is____
questions.solution:
For a weak acid,
$\mathrm{pH}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\mathrm{p} K_{\mathrm{a}}-\log C\right]$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{3}\left[4.74-\log 10^{-2}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \times 6.74=3.37\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Determine the pH of 0.024 M hydroxylamine hydrochloride solution. ( $K_b$ of hydroxyl amine is $10^{-8}$ ).
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{HONH}_3^{+}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{HONH}_3 \mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{H}^{+}$
$\begin{aligned} K_h & =\frac{K_w}{k_b}=\frac{10^{-14}}{10^{-8}}=10^{-6} \\ {\left[H^{+}\right] } & =\sqrt{K_h C} \\ & =\sqrt{10^{-6} \times 0.024} \\ & =1.5 \times 10^{-4}\end{aligned}$
and $\mathrm{pH}=3.8$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Strongest conjugate base is
questions.solution:
If acid is weak, its conjugate base is strong. Greater the oxidation number of Cl , stronger the acid and thus, weaker the conjugate base. $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{HClO} \mathrm{HClO}_2 \mathrm{HClO}_3 \mathrm{HClO}_4 \end{aligned}$
Oxidation number of Cl and acid strength increases $\mathrm{ClO}^{-} \mathrm{ClO}_2^{-} \mathrm{ClO}_3^{-} \mathrm{ClO}_4^{-}$
Conjugate base strength increases. Hence, $\mathrm{ClO}^{-}$is the strongest base.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Determine solubility of AgBr in a 0.1 M KCN solution. $\left[K_f\right.$ of $\left[\mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{CN})_2\right]^{-}=5.6 \times 10^8 ; K_{\mathrm{sp}}$ of $\left.\mathrm{AgBr}=7.7 \times 10^{-13}\right]$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{AgBr}+2 \mathrm{CN}^{-} \rightleftharpoons \underset{\mathrm{s}}{\mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{CN})_2^{-}}+\mathrm{Br}_{\mathrm{s}}^{-}$
$\begin{aligned} 0.1-2 s K_{\text {sp }} & =k_{\text {sp }} \cdot k=4.3 \times 10^{-4} \\ 4.3 \times 10^{-4} & =\left(\frac{s}{0.1-2 s}\right) \\ s & =2 \times 10^{-3}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is tetrabasic acid?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ (pyrophosphoric acid) is tetrabasic because it gives four $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If $K_a$ of $\mathrm{HCN}=4 \times 10^{-10}$, then the pH of $2.5 \times 10^{-1}$ molar $\mathrm{HCN}(\mathrm{aq})$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} {\left[\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}\right] } & =\sqrt{K_a \times C} \\ & =\sqrt{4 \times 10^{-10} \times 2.5 \times 10^{-1}} \\ & =\sqrt{10^{-10}}=10^{-5} \mathrm{M} \\ \because \quad \mathrm{pH} & =-\log \left[\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}\right] \\ \therefore \quad \mathrm{pH} & =-\log \left(10^{-5}\right)=5\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The nature of $\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is amphoteric in nature as it reacts with both acid and alkali.
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\underset{\text { acid }}{6 \mathrm{HCl}} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeCl}_3+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\underset{\text { alkali }}{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaFeO}_2+\mathrm{CO}_2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The pH of a solution is increased from 3 to 6 , its $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ion concentration will be
questions.solution: When $\mathrm{pH}=3$ then $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$. After that we increased the pH from 3 to 6 then $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=10^{-6} \mathrm{M}$ means reduced 1000 times.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Solubility product of $\mathrm{BaCl}_2$ is $4 \times 10^{-9}$. Its solubility in $\mathrm{mol} / \mathrm{L}$ would be
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}\mathrm{BaCl}_2 \rightleftharpoons \stackrel{\substack{2+\\ \mathrm{Ba} \\ s}}{ } + 2\underset{2 \mathrm{~s}}{\mathrm{Cl}}^{-} K_{\mathrm{sp}}=4 s^3, \\ s^3=\frac{4 \times 10^{-9}}{4}=10^{-3} \\ \\ s=10^{-3} \mathrm{M}\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An example of a Lewis acid is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ in a lewis acid because it is a electron pair acceptor, its central atom have a vacant $d$-orbital.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The pH of a neutral water sample is 6.5 . Then the temperature of water
options: can be more or less than $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
questions.solution: pH varies inversely with temperature. Thus, if the pH of neutral water is 6.5 , the temperature of water is more than $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which is Lewis acid?
questions.solution: Electron acceptors are Lewis acids. They are electron deficient compounds. $\mathrm{BF}_3$ is Lewis acid because B has only 6 electrons in its valence shell and it can accept electrons. $\mathrm{NF}_3, \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ have lone pair of electrons. Thus, they are electron donors and Lewis bases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The pH of $10^{-10} \mathrm{M}$ $\mathrm{NaOH}$ solution is nearest to
questions.solution:
Given, concentration of $\mathrm{NaOH}=10^{-10} \mathrm{M}$
$\underset{10^{-10}}{\mathrm{NaOH}} \longrightarrow \underset{10^{-10}}{\mathrm{Na}^{+}}+\underset{10^{-10}}{\mathrm{OH}^{-}}$
$\therefore [\mathrm{OH}]^{-}$from $\mathrm{NaOH}=10^{-10}$
$\therefore$ Total $\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]=10^{-7}+10^{-10}$
$=10^{-7}(1+0.001)$
$=10^{-7}\left(\frac{1001}{1000}\right)$
$=10^{-10} \times 1001$
$\mathrm{pOH}=-\log \left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]$
$=-\log \left(1001 \times 10^{-10}\right)$
$=-3.004+10$
$=6.9996$
$\because \mathrm{pH}+\mathrm{pOH}=14$
$\therefore  \mathrm{pH}=14-6.9996$
$=7.0004 \approx 7$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The solubility of AgCl in 0.2 M NaCl solution is $\left(K_{\mathrm{sp}}\right.$ of $\left.\mathrm{AgCl}=1.20 \times 10^{-10}\right)$
questions.solution:
Given, concentration of $\mathrm{NaCl}=0.2 \mathrm{M}$ 
$K_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgCl})=1.20 \times 10^{-10}$
Let the solubility of AgCl in $\mathrm{NaCl}=x$ 
$\mathrm{AgCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$
Solubility $\quad  \stackrel{x}{\underset{0.2}{\mathrm{NaCl}}}  \longrightarrow \stackrel{x}{\underset{0.2}{\mathrm{Na}^{+}}}+\stackrel{x}{\underset{0.2}{\mathrm{Cl}^{-}}}$
$\therefore \quad\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]=x$ and $\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]=(x+0.2)$
$\therefore \quad K_{\text {sp }}(\mathrm{AgCl})=\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]$
$\begin{aligned} & =x(x+0.2) \\ & =x^2+0.2 x\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad K_{\text {sp }}=0.2 x\left(x^2\lt \lt 1\right)$
or $\quad 1.2 \times 10^{-10}=0.2 x$
$\therefore \quad x=6 \times 10^{-10}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following plot represents the graph of pH against volume of alkali added in the titration of NaOH and HCl ?
questions.solution:
If pH is less then 7, the solution is acidic but if the pH is more than 7 the solution becomes basic.
$0 \text { Acid } 7 \text { Base } 1 4$
If alkali is added in acid solution the pH increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{pH}^{\prime}$ of an acid buffer is given by
questions.solution:
pH of an acid buffer is given by Henderson equation.
$\mathrm{pH}=-\log K_a+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]}$
or $\quad \mathrm{pH}=\mathrm{p} K_a+\log \frac{[\text { Salt }]}{[\text { Acid }]}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The solubility product of $\mathrm{Al}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$ is given by the expression
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{Al}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3 & \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{Al}^{3+}+3 \mathrm{SO}_4{ }^{2-} \\ K_{\mathrm{sp}} & =\left[\mathrm{Al}^{3+}\right]^2\left[\mathrm{SO}_4{ }^{2-}\right]^3\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If 200 mL of a 0.031 molar solution of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ are added to 84 mL of a 0.150 M KOH solution, what is the pH of the resulting solution?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{mmol} \text { of } \mathrm{H}^{+} \text {(initial) }=200 \times 0.031 \times 2=12.4 \\ & \mathrm{mmol} \text { of } \mathrm{OH}^{-} \text {(initial) }=84 \times 0.15=12.6 \\ & \mathrm{mmol} \text { of } \mathrm{OH}^{-}(\text {left }) \text { after neutralisation }=0.2 \\ & {\left[\mathrm{OH}^{-}\right]_{\text {final }}=\frac{0.2}{284}=7 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{M}} \\ & \mathrm{pOH}=3.15 \text { and } \mathrm{pH}=10.85\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Solution of $0.1 \mathrm{~N} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ and $0.1 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ has pH 9.25. Then $\mathrm{pK} \mathrm{K}_b$ of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{pOH}=\mathrm{p} K_b+\log \frac{[\text { salt }]}{[\text { base }]}=p K_b+0 \\ & \mathrm{pK}_b=\mathrm{pOH}=14-\mathrm{pH}=14-9.25=4.75\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is known as Glauber's salt?
questions.solution: \begin{array}{l|l}\hline {c|}{ Formula } & {c}{ Name } \\\hline (a) \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3 \cdot 5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} & hypo \\(b) \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot 10 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} & Glauber's salt \\(c) \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3 \cdot 10 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} & Washing soda \\(d) \mathrm{Na}\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right) \mathrm{HPO}_4 & Microcosmic salt \\\hline\end{array}

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Purification of NaCl by passage of hydrogen chloride through brine is based on
questions.solution: Purification of NaCl by passage of hydrogen chloride through brine is based on common ion effect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The conjugate base of the acid $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ is
questions.solution:
The conjugate base of the acid $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$ is $\mathrm{HS}^{-}$.
$\underset{\text { Acid }}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}} \rightleftharpoons \underset{\substack{\text { Conjugate } \\ \text { base }}}{\mathrm{HS}^{-}}+\mathrm{H}^{+}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following anions is present in the chain structure of silicates?
questions.solution: In $\left(\mathrm{Si}_2 \mathrm{O}_3^{2-}\right)_n$ two oxygen atoms per $\mathrm{SiO}_4$ tetrahedron are shared giving polymeric anions chain. e.g. Spodumene, $\mathrm{LiAl}\left(\mathrm{SiO}_3\right)_2$ and diposide, $\mathrm{CaMg}\left(\mathrm{SiO}_3\right)_2$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Among boron halides, which is the strongest Lewis is acid?
questions.solution: Generally, as the electronegativity increases, acidic character increases, but in case of boron halides. $\mathrm{BI}_3$ is the most acidic, i.e. strongest Lewis acid. This is because there is back donation of electrons from F -atom to boron in case of $\mathrm{BF}_3$. This makes $\mathrm{BF}_3$ less electron deficient. The tendency of back donation decreases as the size of halogen atom increases due to large energy difference. Hence, $\mathrm{BI}_3$ is the most electron deficient and thus, the strongest Lewis acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ fume in air due to
questions.solution:
Anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ is highly hygroscopic and hence gives fumes of hydrogen chloride by the hydrolysis of moist air.
$\mathrm{AlCl}_3+\underset{\text { moist air }}{3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Al}(\mathrm{OH})_3+3 \mathrm{HCl}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ferric alum has the composition $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3 \cdot x \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ The value of $x$ is
questions.solution: $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \cdot \mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3 \cdot 24 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is a gas, while $\mathrm{SiO}_2$ is a solid, but both are
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{CO}_2$ is a gas, while $\mathrm{SiO}_2$ is a solid but both are acidic in nature, both reacts with base and forms carbonate and silicates.
$\begin{gathered}\mathrm{CO}_2+\underset{\text { Base }}{\mathrm{NaOH}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaHCO}_3 \\ \underset{\text { Base }}{\mathrm{SiO}_2+\underset{\text { BaOH }}{2 \mathrm{NaOH}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SiO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Total number of $2 c-2 e^{-}$and $3 c-3 e^{-}$bonds are present in diborane is___
questions.solution: Number of $3 c-2 e^{-1}$ and $2 c-2 e^{-}$bond present in diborane are 2 and 4, respectively. Thus, total number of $3 c-2 e^{-}$and $2 c-2 e^{-}$bonds are 6.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Diborane on reaction with olefins forms :
questions.solution: Diborane readily adds at $0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to the olefin or acetylenic compound to form trialkyl boranes. This reaction is known as hydro-boration. $\underset{\text { Ethylene }}{6 \mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2}+\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6 \xrightarrow{\text { ether }}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The relative Lewis acid character of boron trihalides is the order
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{BI}_3\gt\mathrm{BBr}_3\gt\mathrm{BCl}_3\gt\mathrm{BF}_3$
This order can be easily explained on the basis of the tendaney of the halogen atom to back donate its lone pair of electrones to empty p-orbital of the boron atom through $p \pi-p \pi$ bonding.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{SiF}_4$ gets hydrolysed giving
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{SiF}_4+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Silicic acid }}{\mathrm{Si}(\mathrm{OH})_4}+4 \mathrm{HCl}$
The trend towards hydrolysis decreases down the group. Silicon have vacant orbital to which water molecules can co-ordinate and hence their halides are hydrolysed by water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Thermodynamically, in which form of carbon is the most stable?
questions.solution: Thermodynamically, graphite is the most stable form of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the following reaction $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{S} \rightarrow A+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; A$ is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{NaOH}+2 \mathrm{~S} \underset{\text { Sodium sulphite }}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_3+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Which of the following does not exist?
I. $\mathrm{HO}_3 \mathrm{~S}-\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{SO}_3 \mathrm{H}$
II. $\mathrm{HO}-\mathrm{Te}-\mathrm{OH}$
III. $\mathrm{HS}-\mathrm{S}_2-\mathrm{SH}$
IV. HS-Po-OH
questions.solution:
I. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_3 \mathrm{O}_6$ - trithionic acid is an example of polythionic acid.
III. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_4$ or $\mathrm{HS}-\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{SH}$ is an hydrosulfane.
The compounds (II) and (IV) do not exist.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ozone when react with potassium iodide solution liberates certain product, which turns starch paper blue. The liberated substance is :
questions.solution: When potassium iodide react with ozone it oxidise Kl and liberate $\mathrm{I}_2$ it turns starch paper blue.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $F_2$ is formed by reacting $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{MnF}_6$ with
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{MnF}_6$ react with $\mathrm{SbF}_5$ to form $\mathrm{F}_2$. In this reaction stronger Lewis acid $\mathrm{SbF}_5$ displace the weaker one $\left(\mathrm{MnF}_4\right)$ from its salt. $\mathrm{MnF}_4$ being unstable, readily decompose to give $\mathrm{MnF}_3$ and fluorine.
The complete reaction is as follows, $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{MnF}_6+2 \mathrm{SbF}_5 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KSbF}_6+\mathrm{MnF}_4$ (unstable)
$2 \mathrm{MnF}_4 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{MnF}_3+\mathrm{F}_2$
Overall reaction,
$2 \mathrm{~K}_2 \mathrm{MnF}_6+4 \mathrm{SbF}_5 \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{KSbF}_6+2 \mathrm{MnF}_3+\mathrm{F}_2$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Pure cold conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ makes iron passive, as the surface is covered with protective layer of
questions.solution: Cold conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ makes it passive in which state it fails to exhibit the properties of normal iron. Passivity is said to be due to the deposit of a thin layer of iron oxide $\left(\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4\right)$ on the surface.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ozone when reacts with potassium iodide solution liberates certain product, which turns starch paper blue. The liberated substance is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{O}_3$ (Ozone) works as oxidising agent. It reacts with potassium iodide (KI) solution and liberate iodine vapours that turns starch paper blue.
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow & \mathrm{O}_2+\mathrm{O} \\ & \xrightarrow[2 \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{I}_2]{2 \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KOH}+\mathrm{I}_2+\mathrm{O}_2}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Red hot iron absorbs $\mathrm{SO}_2$ giving the product.
questions.solution:
Red hot iron absorbs $\mathrm{SO}_2$ and give FeO and FeS.
$\mathrm{Fe}+\mathrm{SO}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{FeO}+\mathrm{FeS}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The noble gas used for treatment of cancer is
questions.solution: Radon is radioactive with half-life period of 3.8 days and is used for the treatment of cancer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The first compound of noble gas prepared by Bartlett was
questions.solution: The first ever compound of noble gas $\mathrm{Xe}^{+}\left[\mathrm{PtF}_6\right]^{-}$was prepared by Neil Bartlett in 1962.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The gas mixture which is used by the divers inside the sea is
questions.solution: Helium-oxygen mixture is used by deep sea divers because of its very low solubility in blood.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Hydrolysis of $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ gives $\mathrm{NH}_2$ and X . Which of the following is $X$ ?
questions.solution:
Hydrolysis of $\mathrm{NCl}_3$ gives $\mathrm{NH}_3$ or $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ and HClO as
$\mathrm{NCl}_3+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}+3 \mathrm{HOCl}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The high oxidising power of fluorine in due to its
options: high heat of dissociation and low heat of hydration
low heat of dissociation and high heat of hydration
high heat of dissociation and high heat of hydration
questions.solution: The high oxidising power of fluorine is due to its low heat of dissociation and high heat of hydration. Due to its smallest size, there is considerable repulsion in the non-bonding electrons which weakens the F-F bond. Thus, bond dissociation energy of $F_2$ is low.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Solid $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is
questions.solution: Solid $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is ionic. X-ray diffraction shows that solid $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ exists as $\mathrm{NO}_2^{+} \mathrm{NO}_3^{-}$(nitronium nitrate).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following phosphorus is the most reactive?
questions.solution: White phosphorus is highly reactive, bursting into flames when exposed to air, and is thus stored under water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{ClO}_2$ is an/a
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{ClO}_2$ reacts with KOH forming $\mathrm{KCl}_3$ and $\mathrm{KClO}_2$
$2 \mathrm{KOH}+2 \mathrm{ClO}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{KClO}_2+\mathrm{KClO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
This reaction indicates that $\mathrm{ClO}_2$ is a mixed anhydride of $\mathrm{HClO}_2$ and $\mathrm{HClO}_3$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Complete the following reaction,
$\mathrm{P}_4+\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \text { ? }$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following statments about NO is wrong?
options: NO is an odd electron molecule
It is a free radical and highly reactive
It readily forms complexes with transition metal ions
It can be prepared by heating $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_3$ at $250^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
questions.solution: It can be prepared in the laboratory by reacting sodium, nitrite $\left(\mathrm{NaNO}_2\right)$ with a reducing agent such as $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ in an acidic medium. $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_3$ on heating at $250^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ gives $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
$(A)+$ tap water $\rightarrow$ white turbidity soluble in $a q$. $\mathrm{NH}_3$
$(A) \xrightarrow{\Delta}$ residue $(B)+\mathrm{NO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2$
Aqueous $(A)$ gives brown ring on adding $\mathrm{FeSO}_4$ and Conc. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$. Identify $(A)$.
questions.solution:
$(A)+$ tap water $\rightarrow$ white turbidity soluble in aq. $\mathrm{NH}_3$.
Tap water has $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$and turbidity is soluble in aq $\mathrm{NH}_3$.
Turbidity is of AgCl and thus $(A)$ has $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$
(A) also gives ring test of $\mathrm{NO}_3^{-}$
$(A)$ has nitrate and thus $(A)$ is $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the oxyacids of chlorine, $\mathrm{Cl}-\mathrm{O}$ bond contains
questions.solution: In oxyacids of chlorine, $\mathrm{HClO}, \mathrm{HClO}_2, \mathrm{HClO}_3$ and $\mathrm{HClO}_4$, the central Cl atom is $s p^3$ hybridised. The half filled unhybridised $d$-orbitals of Cl and $p$-orbitals of oxygen overlap to form $\pi$-bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the following reaction, $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{S} \longrightarrow A+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; A$ is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{S} \longrightarrow \underset{\text {'A' }}{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_3}+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Ammonia is dried over
questions.solution:
Quick lime, CaO is used to dry ammonia, as with other given dehydrating agents ammonia reacts.
$4 \mathrm{NH}_3+\mathrm{CaCl}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCl}_2 \cdot 4 \mathrm{NH}_3$
$4 \mathrm{NH}_3+2 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_5 \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{PO}_3$
$\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ is never used as dehydrating agent.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Neon atom requires energy to remove one electron from its outermost orbit, so also to add one electron into the outermost orbit.
Which of the following is correct about fluorine?
I. Fluorine releases energy when an electron is removed.
II. Fluorine requires energy to add one electron.
questions.solution:
(i) Fluorine required energy when an electron is removed, i.e. ionisation energy.
(ii) Fluorine releases energy to add one electron, i.e. electron affinity $\left(-328 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Out of $\mathrm{N}, \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Ar}, \mathrm{Sb}$ and Bi , the number of elements that form pentahalides are $\qquad$
questions.solution: Nitrogen does not form pentahalide due to non-availability of the $d$-orbitals in its valence shell.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In group 15 elements which element show a fractional oxidation state?
questions.solution: In group number 15 elements shows -III and III oxidation state (by gain or loss of three $p$ electrons). These shows V oxidation state due to loss of $n p^3+n s^2$ electrons. But nitrogen is unique in having a large number of oxidation states including a fractional one (i.e., $-\frac{1}{3}$ in $\left.\mathrm{N}_3 \mathrm{H}\right)$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which statement is false for white phosphorus $\left(\mathrm{P}_4\right) ?$
options: It has four lone pairs of electrons
questions.solution: White phosphorus $\left(P_4\right)$ It has six $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ single bonds.

questions.subject: Chemistry
options: $\mathrm{O}^{-}$ion has comparatively larger size than oxygen atom
$\mathrm{O}^{-}$ion will tend to resist the addition of another electron
questions.solution: The formation of oxide ion $\mathrm{O}^{2-}(\mathrm{g})$ require first an exothermic and then an endothermic step because the $\mathrm{O}^{-}$ion will tend to resist the addition of another electron.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is the strongest oxidising agent?
questions.solution: Perchloric acid $\left(\mathrm{HClO}_4\right)$ is the strongest acid among these acidic because the acidic character of oxoacid increases with increasing the oxidation number of a particular halogen atom.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{I}_2$ dissolve in KI solution due to formation of
questions.solution: $\mathrm{I}_2$ form complex ion $\Gamma_3$ in KI solution due to which it dissolve in it.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Sulphuric acid has great affinity for water because
questions.solution: $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ forms hydrate with water. That's why it has great affinity towards water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: PDI for natural polymers is generally close to
questions.solution:
PDI abbreviates as polydispersity index of polymer.
$\mathrm{PDI}=\frac{\bar{M}_w}{M_n}$
For natural polymers, $\mathrm{PDI}=1$
and for synthetic polymers, PDI $\gt1$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one is chain growth polymer?
questions.solution:
Chain growth polymers are formed by the chain growth polymerisation or chain polymerisation. This polymerisation process involves in a series of reaction each of which consumes a relative particles and produces another similar particle resulting a chain reaction. Teflon is a chain growth polymer. It is the polymer of tetraflouro ethylene.
$\left.\underset{\text { Tetrofluoro ethylene }}{n\left(\mathrm{CF}_2\right.}=\mathrm{CF}_2\right) \xrightarrow[\text { Pressure }]{\text { High }}-\left(\mathrm{CF}_2 \underset{\text { Teflon }}{\left.-\mathrm{CF}_2\right)_n}\right.$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Natural rubber is a polymer of monomer isoprene. During polymerisaton
options: both double bonds are converted into single bond
questions.solution: Natural rubber is a linear polymers of isoprene and is also called as cis-1, 4-polyisoprene. It is a linear 1, 4-polymer of isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Nylon threads are made of
questions.solution: Nylon threads are made up of polyamide. Some common examples are nylon 6 and nylon 66.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Polystyrene is a ______ polymer whereas dacron is a _____ polymer.
questions.solution:
Polystyrene is an addition and chain growth polymer. It is thermoplastic. Addition polymers have unsaturated monomers.
Dacron is a condensation and step-growth polymer. It is a fibre. Condensation polymerisation involves a repetitive condensation reaction between two bi-functional or tri-functional monomeric units.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Acrilan is a hard, horny and a high melting material. Its structure is :
questions.solution: Acrilan is a cyanide compound. It has a structure

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In which of the given form styrene exist at room temperature?
questions.solution: Styrene exist as liquid at room temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: What is incorrect regarding natural rubber?
options: Molecules of rubber have coiled conformation
questions.solution: Rubber has isotactic stereochemistry, not syndiotactic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following polymers can be used for lubrication and as an insulator?
questions.solution: Poly tetrafluoro ethylene (PTFE) or teflon $\left.+\mathrm{CF}_2-\mathrm{CF}_2\right)_n$ is an addition homopolymer. It is used as lubricant, insulator and in making cooking wares.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following is the structure of polyacrylonitrile?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An aqueous solution contains following ions: $\mathrm{Hg}_2^{2+}, \mathrm{Hg}^{2+}, \mathrm{Pb}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Cd}^{2+}$. On adding HCl , which of these will precipitate?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{Hg}_2^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Pb}^{2+}$ have been placed in I group of basic radicals as their $K_{\text {sp }}$ are less than the II group ions $\left(\mathrm{Hg}^{2+}, \mathrm{Cd}^{2+}\right)$. Hence, only these will precipitate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{K}_2\left[\mathrm{Hgl}_4\right]$ detects the following ion
questions.solution: Analysis of $\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}$cation Take small amount of salt in the test tube and add NaOH and heat. Add Nessler's reagent $\left[\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{I}_4\right]$ to the solution, brown ppt is obtained. It confirmed $\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}$ion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The reagent $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ and aqueous $\mathrm{NH}_3$ will precipitate :
questions.solution: $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ and aqueous $\mathrm{NH}_3\left(\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}\right)$ precipitates basic radicals of 3rd group of qualitative analysis hydroxides of this group have very low value of solubility product. So, this reagent will precipitate $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}$ because the radicals present in 3rd group are $\mathrm{Al}^{3+}, \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$, and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Brown ring is made for
questions.solution:
Ring test for nitrate ions is
$\mathrm{FeSO}_4+\mathrm{NO}+5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Dark brown ring }}{\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_5 \mathrm{NO} \mathrm{SO}_4\right.}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in
questions.solution: Oxygen has an oxidation state of +2 in $\mathrm{OF}_2$ because oxidation number of fluorine is always $-1$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The oxidation number of N and Cl in $\mathrm{NOClO}_4$ respectively are
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{NOClO}_4 \equiv\left(\mathrm{NO}^{+}\right) \quad\left(\mathrm{ClO}_4{ }^{-}\right)$
Let the O.N. of N in $\mathrm{NO}^{+}$is $x$.
$\begin{aligned}
x+(-2) & =+1 \\
x & =3
\end{aligned}$
Let $\mathrm{O} . \mathrm{N}$. of Cl in $\mathrm{ClO}_4{ }^{-}$is $y$.
$\begin{aligned}
y+4(-2) & =-1 \\
y-8 & =-1 \\
y & =+7
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The oxidation number of cobalt in $\mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CO})_4\right]$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{K}\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CO})_4\right] \\ & +1+x+0 \times 4=0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \qquad x=-1$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Out of the following redox reactions
I. $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_3 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
II. $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{NO}_2 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{~N}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
III. $\mathrm{PCl}_5 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{PCl}_3+\mathrm{Cl}_2$
disproportionation is not shown in
questions.solution:
$\text { I. } \mathrm{NH}_{-3}^{+} \mathrm{NO}_3^{-} \longrightarrow \underset{+1}{\mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}$
It is a redox reaction but not a disproportionation reaction. Since, different N -species are involved. II. $\mathrm{NH}_{-3}^{+}{ }_{+3}^{+} \mathrm{NO}_2{ }^{-} \longrightarrow \underset{0}{\mathrm{~N}_2}$ same as (I) III. $\mathrm{PCl}_5 \longrightarrow \mathrm{PCl}_3 \longrightarrow+\underset{+3-1}{\mathrm{Cl}_2}$
Thus, it is also not a disproportionation reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Transition metal with low oxidation number will act as
questions.solution: Transition metal which have low oxidation number show the oxidising nature because of great tendency to lose the electron.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following species contain an element in an oxidation state that is not a whole number?
questions.solution:
In $\mathrm{S}_4 \mathrm{O}_6{ }^{2-}$, let ' $x$ ' be the oxidation number of S . Thus, the element $X$ may be bromine.
$\begin{aligned}
4 x-12 & =-2 \\
x & =\frac{10}{4}=2.5
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In acid medium $\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$is reduced to $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$, by a reducing agent. Then the equivalent mass of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ is given by ( $M=$ molecular mass $)$
questions.solution: ${ }^{+7} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{+2}$ (Acid medium). Change $\begin{aligned} & \text { in oxidation number }=+5 \text { Equivalent weight } \\ & \begin{aligned} \text { of } \mathrm{KMnO}_4 & =\frac{\text { Molecular weight of } \mathrm{KMnO}_4}{\text { Change in oxidation number }} \\ & =\frac{M}{5}\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is a wrong statement?
options: $\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_4$ has zero oxidation number for Ni
questions.solution: In $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_4\right]$, Ni has zero oxidation number.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Of the following outer electronic configurations of atoms, the highest oxidation state is achieved by which one of them?
questions.solution:
Maximum oxidation state exhibited by $d$ block elements (O.S) = no. of $n s$ electrons + no. of $(n-1) d$ electrons.
(a) O.S. $=2+2=4$
(b) O.S. $=5+1=6$
(c) O.S. $=3+2=5$
(d) O.S. $=5+2=7$
$\therefore(n-1) d^5 n s^2$ configuration will achieve the highest oxidation state.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The equivalent weight of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ for acid solution is
questions.solution:
In acidic medium following reaction takes place.
$8 \mathrm{H}^{+}+5 e^{-}+\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$\therefore$ Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ in acidic medium $=\frac{\text { molecular weight of } \mathrm{KMnO}_4}{5}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{158}{5} \\ & =31.6\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The oxidation state of Fe in $\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4$ is
questions.solution:
Let the oxidation state of Fe in $\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4=x$
$\therefore  3 x+4 \times(-2)=0$
or $3 x-8=0$
$\therefore x=\frac{8}{3}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Carbon cannot be used in the reduction of $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ because
options: the enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is more than that of $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$
pure carbon is not easily available
the enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is too high
questions.solution: The enthalpy of formation of $\mathrm{Al}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ is very high and hence, it is not possible to reduce it by carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
The coefficients of $\mathrm{I}^{-}, \mathrm{IO}_3^{-}$and $\mathrm{H}^{+}$in the red́ox reaction,
$\mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+\mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{I}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
in the balanced form respectively are
questions.solution:
The balanced equation is as
$5 \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+6 \mathrm{H}^{+} \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{I}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the standardization of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ using $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ by iodometry, the equivalent weight of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ is
questions.solution:
Potassium dichromate acts like an oxidising agent in the presence of dilute $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ as:
$\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7{ }^{2-}+14 \mathrm{H}^{+}+6 e^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}+7 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$\therefore$ Equivalent mass of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$
$=\frac{\text { molecular mass of } \mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7}{6}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Concentrated nitric acid $\left(\mathrm{HNO}_3\right)$ oxidises phosphorus to
questions.solution: $\mathrm{P}_4+20 \mathrm{HNO}_3 \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4+20 \mathrm{NO}_2+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Photographic paper is developed with alkaline hydroquinone
Select correct statement.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$is reduced hence, it is oxidant Hydroquinone is oxidised hence, it is reductant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
In the following reaction, which choice has value twice that of the equivalent weight of the oxidising agent?
$\mathrm{SO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \mathrm{S}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
Eq. wt. of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ in the reaction
$=\frac{\text { molar mass of } \mathrm{SO}_2}{\text { change in oxidation state }}=\frac{64}{4}=16$
Twice of 16 will be 32 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Match List I with List II and choose correct answer from the codes given below
$\begin{array}{llll}\mathrm{A} & \mathrm{B} & \mathrm{C} & \mathrm{D}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{KO}_2$ is used in space and submarines because it
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{KO}_2$ absorb $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and increase $\mathrm{O}_2$ concentration.
So, it is used in space and submarines.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The property of the alkaline earth metals which increases with their atomic number?
options: Solubility of their hydroxides in water
Solubility of their sulphates in water
questions.solution: Solubility of alkaline earth metal hydroxide in water increases with increase in atomic number.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The activity of alkaline earth metals as reducing agent
options: increases from Be to Ca and decreases from Ca to Ba
decreases from Be to Ca and increases from Ca to Ba
questions.solution: The activity of alkaline earth metals as reducing agent increases from Be to Ba .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: When $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is passed through solution of calcium hydroxide, which one of the following compound is precipitated?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{CO}_2$ gas is passed in $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ solution the solution becomes milky white to form $\mathrm{CaCO}_3$ and if the $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is passed in excess the milky white solution become colourless due to the formation of $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3\right)_2$.
$\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2+\mathrm{CO}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{CaCO}_3 \downarrow+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: When sodium hydroxide react with sand, it form :
questions.solution:
NaOH when react with sand $\mathrm{SiO}_2$, it forms sod. silicate which is generally called glass.
$2 \mathrm{NaOH}+\mathrm{SiO}_2 \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Sod. silicate (glass) }}{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SiO}_3}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The hydroxide of alkaline earth metal not soluble in water is
questions.solution: As we move down the group, solubility increases. $\mathrm{So}, \mathrm{Be}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ is insoluble and $\mathrm{Ba}(\mathrm{OH})_2$ is highly soluble in water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $\mathrm{KO}_2$ is used in life supports in space crafts, submarines and emergency breathing apparatus since it
questions.solution: It absorbs $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and releases $\mathrm{O}_2$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is the formula of Sorel's cement?
questions.solution: Mixture of $\mathrm{MgCl}_2$ and MgO is called Sorel's cement. It is $\mathrm{MgCl}_2 \cdot 5 \mathrm{MgO} \cdot x \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The first element of rare earth metal is
questions.solution: Cerium is the first element of rare earth metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Beryllium shows diagonal relationship with
questions.solution: The elements of II period show similar properties as the elements of III period, which are diagonally placed to them. This is known as diagonal relationship.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Sodium carbonate is manufactured by :
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ is manufactured by Solvay process.
Lowing process is for the manufacture of NaOH Leblanc process is for the manufacture of $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ Haber's process is for the manufacture of $\mathrm{NH}_3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
In the given reaction, the oxide of sodium is
$\binom{4 \mathrm{Na}+\mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{O}}{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NaOH}}$
questions.solution: Basic Oxide of metals are basic in nature and form basic hydroxide with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Baking powder is more commonly used to make cakes or bread "rise". Filler in baking powder is
questions.solution: An improved combination of baking powder contains about $40 \%$ starch, $30 \% \mathrm{NaHCO}_3, 20 \%$ $\mathrm{NaAl}\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_2$ and $10 \% \mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_4\right)_2$. Here starch acts as filler.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which pair is different from the others?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Li}-\mathrm{Mg} \rightarrow$ diagonal relationship.
$\mathrm{B}-\mathrm{Al}, \mathrm{Na}-\mathrm{K}$ and $\mathrm{Ca}-\mathrm{Mg} \rightarrow$ belongs to same group.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the equilibrium mixture, $\mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{I}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{KI}_3$; the concentration of KI and $\mathrm{I}_2$ is made two fold and three fold respectively. The concentration of $\mathrm{Kl}_3$ becomes
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{KI}+\mathrm{I}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Kl}_3 \text { or } \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{I}_2 \underset{\text { from } \mathrm{KI}}{\longrightarrow} \\ & \mathrm{I}_3^{-}\end{aligned}$
On increasing concentration of KI and $\mathrm{I}_2$ two fold and three fold respectively, the concentration of $\mathrm{Kl}_3$ becomes two fold.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Semiconductors are derived from compounds of
questions.solution: Semiconductor are derived from compounds of $p$-block elements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If ionic radius of $\mathrm{Cs}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$are $1.69 Å$ and $1.81 Å$ respectively, the edge length of unit cell is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 2\left(r^{+}+r^{-}\right)=\sqrt{3} a \\ & 2(1.69+1.81)=\sqrt{3} a \\ & 2(3.5)=\sqrt{3} a \\ & 7.0=\sqrt{3} a\end{aligned}$
$a=\frac{7.0}{\sqrt{3}}=4.04 Å$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The crystalline structure of NaCl is
questions.solution: Sodium chloride $(\mathrm{NaCl})$ has face centred cubic structure. It contains $4 \mathrm{Na}^{+}$and $4 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ions. Each $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$ ion is surrounded by $8 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ ions and each $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ ion is also surrounded by $8 \mathrm{Na}^{+}$ ions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Find the pressure of a sample of $\mathrm{CCl}_4$ if 1.0 mole occupies 35 L at $77^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Assume $\mathrm{CCl}_4$ obeys the van der Waals equation of state. $\left(a=20 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~mol}^2 \mathrm{~atm}^{-2}\right.$ and $\left.b=0.14 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution: Pressure, $\begin{aligned} p & =\frac{R T}{V-b}-\frac{a}{V^2} \\ & =\frac{0.082 \times 350}{35-0.14}-\frac{20}{(35)^2} \\ & =0.807 \mathrm{~atm}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Coordination number of Zn in ZnS is
questions.solution: In $\mathrm{ZnS}, \mathrm{S}^{-}$ions adopt fcc lattice in which $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}$ ions are present at alternate tetrahedral sites. Hence, coordination number of $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ is equal to coordination number of $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}$ i.e. 4 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is not correct for ionic crystals?
options: They possess high melting point and boiling point
Exhibit directional properties of the bond
questions.solution: Ionic crystals have no directional bonds as the oppositely charged ions are attracted by electrostatic forces and arranged in a definite ratio.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The flame colours of metal ions are due to
questions.solution: Flame colours of metal ions are due to metal excess defect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Addition of arsenic to germanium makes the latter:
questions.solution: Since, As is added from outside, it is an extrinsic semiconductor.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The maximum proportion of available volume that can be filled by hard spheres in diamond is
questions.solution: In diamond, the maximum proportion of available volume that can be filled by hard spheres $=\frac{\pi \sqrt{3}}{16}$ $=0.34$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If we mix a pentavalent impurity in a crystal lattice of germanium, what type of semiconductor formation will occur?
questions.solution: On adding a pentavalent impurity with germanium, we get $n$-type of semiconductors because excess of electrons is responsible for conduction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In a face-centred cubic arrangement of $A$ and $B$ atoms whose $A$ atoms are at the corner of the unit cell and $B$ atoms at the face-centre, one of $A$ atom is missing from one corner in the unit cell. The simplest formula of the compound is
questions.solution:
No. of atoms of $A$ from corners of unit cell $=\frac{7}{8}$ 
No. of atoms of $B$ from faces of unit cell $=3$
$\therefore \qquad A: B=\frac{7}{8}: 3=7: 24$
Thus, formula of the compound is $A_7 B_{24}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In a face centred cubic lattice a unit cell is shared equally by how many unit cells ?
questions.solution: In a face centred cubic lattice, a unit cell is shared equally by six unit cells.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which is mismatched for NaCl crystal?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In CsCl type structure, the coordination number of $\mathrm{Cs}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$respectively are
questions.solution: The coordination number is $8: 8$ in $\mathrm{Cs}^{+}: \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: KBr crystallises in NaCl type of crystal lattice and its density is $2.75 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cm}^3$. Number of unit cells of KBr present in a $1.00 \mathrm{~mm}^3$ grain of KBr are
questions.solution:
In a unit cell of KBr , there are four formula units of KBr therefore, $\text { Density }(\rho)=\frac{4 \times 119}{6.023 \times 10^{23} \times a^3}=2.75$ $\begin{aligned} a^3 & =2.873 \times 10^{-22} \mathrm{~cm}^3 \\ & =2.778 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~mm}^3 \end{aligned}$
Number of unit cells per $\mathrm{mm}^3$ $=\frac{10^{19}}{2.873}=3.5 \times 10^{18}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
An aqueous solution freezes at $-0.186^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, then elevation in boiling point is
$\left(K_b=0.512, K_f=1.86\right)$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $40 \%$ by weight solution will contain how much mass of the solute in 1 L solution, density of the solution is $1.2 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mL}$ ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
The osmotic pressure of 0.2 molar solution of urea at 300 K is
$\left(R=0.082 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution: $\pi=\frac{n}{V} R T=\frac{0.2 \times 0.082 \times 300}{1.0}=4.92 \mathrm{~atm}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If in the reaction, $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4 \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{NO}_2 ; \alpha$ is the degree of dissociation of $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$, then total number of moles at equilibrium is
questions.solution: Total number of moles at equilibrium $=(1-\alpha)+2 \alpha=1+\alpha$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: When attraction between $A-B$ is more than that of $A-A$ and $B-B$, the solution will show
questions.solution: When $A-B$ attractions are greater than $A-A$ and $B-B$, then less vapours are formed and hence, solution shows negative deviation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: To a 4 L of 0.2 M solution of NaOH 2 L of 0.5 M NaOH are added. The molarity of resulting solution is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {Total moles of } \mathrm{NaOH}=4 \times 0.2+2 \times 0.5 \\ & \\ & =0.8+1.0=1.8 \\ & \begin{aligned} \text { Total volume } & =4+2=6 \mathrm{~L} \\ \therefore \text { Molarity } & =\frac{1.8}{6} \\ & =0.3 \mathrm{M}\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following solutions exhibits the maximum elevation in boiling point?
questions.solution: Greater the number of ions, greater will be elevation in boiling point. Hence, $0.1 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{FeCl}_3$ exhibits the maximum elevation in boiling point among these.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Boiling point of water at 750 mm Hg is $99.63^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The amount of sucrose that should be added to 500 g of water such that it boil at $100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is ______ g. (nearest integer)
(Molar elevation constant for water $=0.5 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{mol}$ )
questions.solution:
Elevation in boiling point of water required $\left(\Delta T_b\right)$.
$=100-99.63=0.37{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
Mass of solvent (water), $W_1=500 \mathrm{~g}$
Molar mass of solvent, $M_1=18 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$
Molar mass of solute (sucrose)
$\begin{array}{r}
\left(\mathrm{C}_{12} \mathrm{H}_{22} \mathrm{O}_{11}\right)=342 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol} \\
M_2=\frac{1000 \times K_b \times w_2}{w_1 \Delta T_b}
\end{array}$
or $w_2=\frac{M_2 \times W_1 \times \Delta T_b}{1000 \times K_b}=\frac{342 \times 500 \times 0.37}{1000 \times 0.5}$
$w_2=126.54 \approx 127$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Calculate the molal depression constant of a solvent which has freezing point $16.6^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and latent heat of fusion $180.75 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g}$.
questions.solution:
Given,
$\begin{aligned} T_{\mathrm{f}} & =273+16.6=289.6 \mathrm{~K} \\ L_f & =180.75 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g} \\ R & =8.314 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{K} / \mathrm{mol}\end{aligned}$
$K_f=$ ?,
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & K_f=\frac{R T_f^2}{1000 \times L_f}=\frac{8.314 \times(289.6)^2}{1000 \times 180.75} \\ \therefore & K_f=3.86\end{array}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If the osmotic pressure of a 0.010 M aqueous solution of sucrose at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is 0.25 atm , then the osmotic pressure of a 0.010 M aqueous solution of NaCl at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 0.01 M solution of KCl and $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ are prepared in water. The freezing point of KCl is found to be $-2^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. What is the freezing point of $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ to be completely ionised?
questions.solution:
$i$ for $\mathrm{KCl}=2, i$ for for $\mathrm{CaCl}_2=3$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\Delta T_f(\mathrm{KCl})}{\Delta T_f \propto i}=\frac{2}{\Delta T_f\left(\mathrm{CaCl}_2\right)}=\frac{2}{3}\end{aligned}$
$\Delta T_f\left(\mathrm{CaCl}_2\right)=\frac{3}{2} \times 2=3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$
Freezing point of $\mathrm{CaCl}_2=-3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 10 g of non-volatile solute is dissolved in 180 g of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{Oresulting}$ in lowering of vapour pressure by $0.5 \%$. Determine the boiling point of solution if $K_b$ of water is $0.52 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
questions.solution:
$p=p^{\circ} x_1$
$99.50=100 \frac{n_1}{n_1+n_2}$
$\frac{n_2}{n_1}=\frac{1}{191}$
$\Delta T_b=k_b m=K_b \frac{n_2}{n_1} \times \frac{1000}{m_1}$
$\begin{aligned} & =0.52 \times \frac{1}{191} \times \frac{1000}{18}=0.15 \\ T_b & =100.15^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A 0.15 molal solution of $\mathrm{K}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]$ in water freezes at $-0.65^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. What is the apparent percentage of dissociation of this compound in this solution? $\left(K_f\right.$ for water $\left.=1.86^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} 0.65 & =i \times 1.86 \times 0.15 \\ i & =2.33=1+4 \alpha \\ \alpha & =-0.33\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If in a solvent, $n$ simple molecules of solute combine to form an associated molecule, $\alpha$ is the degree of association, the van't Hoff's factor is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \underset{(1-\alpha)}{n A} \rightleftharpoons & A_{\frac{\alpha}{n}} \\ & i=1-\alpha+\frac{\alpha}{n}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following will represent the normality of $10 \%(w / V)$ acetic acid?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{Normality,} \qquad N & =\frac{w}{E \cdot V} \\ & =\frac{10 \mathrm{~g}}{60 \times \frac{100}{1000} \mathrm{~L}} \\ & =1.66 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The elevation in boiling point would be highest for
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{ll}
\because & \Delta T_b=\frac{1000 \cdot K_b \cdot w}{m \cdot W} \times i \\
\therefore & \Delta T_b \propto i \cdot \frac{w}{m \cdot \frac{W}{1000}}\left(\because K_b=\text { constant }\right)
\end{array}$
or $\quad \Delta T_b \propto i \cdot M$
$\begin{aligned}
(\because \text { Molality } & =\frac{w}{m \cdot \frac{W}{1000}} \text { and assuming, } \\
& \text { Molarity, } M=\text { molality, } m)
\end{aligned}$
Now, for the given solutions,
(a) For $0.08 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{BaCl}_2, i \cdot M=3 \times 0.08=0.24$
(b) For $0.15 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KCl}, i \cdot M=2 \times 0.15=0.30$
(c) For 0.10 M glucose, $i \cdot M=1 \times 0.10=0.10$
(d) For $0.06 \mathrm{MCa}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2, i \cdot M=3 \times 0.06$
$=0.18$
Hence, $\Delta T_b$ is maximum for 0.15 M KCl solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The osmotic pressure of a $5 \%$ (wt./vol.) solution of cane sugar at $150^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} C=\frac{5}{342} & \times \frac{1}{100} \times 1000=\frac{50}{342} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1} \\ \pi & =\frac{50}{342} \times 0.082 \times 423 \\ & =5.07 \mathrm{~atm}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The relative lowering of vapour pressure of a dilute aqueous solution containing non volatile solute is 0.0125 . The molality of the solution is about
questions.solution:
Relative lowering of vapour pressure $=$ mole fraction of solute (Raoult's law)
$\frac{p-p_S}{p}=\chi_2$
$\frac{p-p_S}{p}=\frac{w M}{m W}$
$0.0125=\frac{w M}{m W}$
or$\frac{w}{m W}=\frac{0.0125}{18}=0.00070$
Hence, molality $=\frac{w}{m W} \times 1000$
$=0.0007 \times 1000=0.70$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A $5 \%$ solution of a substance is isotonic with a $1.5 \%$ solution of urea (molar mass $=60 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ) in the same solvent. If the densities of both the solutions are assumed to be equal to $1 \mathrm{gcm}^{-3}$ molar mass of the substance will be____
questions.solution:
Solution with the same osmotic pressure are isotonic.
Let the molar mass of the substance be $M$.
$\therefore \quad \pi_1=\pi_2$
$C_1 R T=C_2 R T$
$C_1=C_2$
As density are also same
So, $\frac{5}{M}=\frac{1.5}{60}$
$\Rightarrow \quad M=\frac{5 \times 60}{1.5}=200 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In case of positive deviation from Raoult's law, the intermolecular attractive forces between the solute-solvent molecules as compared to those between the solute-solute and solvent-solvent molecules are
options: independent of intermolecular forces between solute-solvent molecules.
questions.solution:
In case of positive deviation from Raoult's law, the intermolecular attractive forces between the solute-solvent molecules are weaker than those
between the solute-solute and solvent-solvent molecules. Therefore, it becomes easy for the solute/solvent molecules to escape from the solution than in pure state. This will increase the vapour pressure and results in positive deviation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: van't Hoff factor, when benzoic acid is dissolved in benzene, will be
questions.solution:
When benzoic acid dissolve in benzene, it will be dimerise due to the intermolecular hydrogen bonding. $2 \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{COOH} \xrightarrow{\text { Benzene }}\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{COOH}\right)_2$
$=\frac{1}{2}=0.5$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The concentration of a solution can be expressed in molarity, normality, formality and molality. Among them, which mode of expression is the most accurate for the all conditions?
questions.solution:
Since, molarity normality and formality are calculated against the volume of the solution and the volume of the solution changes with change in
temperature. Therefore, these qualities do not remain constant with temperature.
$\text { Molality }(m)=\frac{\text { Moles of solute }}{\text { Mass of solvent (in kg) }}$
The molality of a solution remains independent of temperature, because it involves only mass, which is independent of temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The relative lowering of vapour pressure of an aqueous solution containing a non volatile solute is 0.0125 . The molality of the solution is
questions.solution:
According to Raoult's law, "Relative lowering of vapour pressure $=$ mole fraction of solute"
$\left(X_B\right)=0.0125$
$\begin{array}{rr}\because & X_B=\frac{m \cdot M_A}{10000+m \cdot M_A} \\ \therefore & 0.0125=\frac{m \times 18}{1000+m \times 18} \\ 12.5+0.225 m=18 m \\ & 17.775 m=12.5 \\ & m=\frac{12.5}{17.775}=0.70\end{array}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: At $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, the highest osmotic pressure is exhibited by 0.1 M solution of
questions.solution:
Osmotic pressure is a colligative property ie, depends only upon the number of particles or ions in solution. More the number of ions in solution, more will be the osmotic pressure of solution.
(i) 0.1 M urea and 0.1 M glucose will have same number of molecules in solution as they don't ionise.
(ii) $\mathrm{KCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{K}^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$(2 ions)
(iii) $\mathrm{CaCl}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}$(3 ions)
$\therefore \mathrm{CaCl}_2$ produces maximum number of ions.
$\therefore$ It will have highest osmotic pressure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Helium-oxygen mixture is used by deep sea divers in preference to nitrogen-oxygen mixture because
options: helium is much less soluble in blood than nitrogen
nitrogen is much less soluble in blood than helium
due to high pressure deep under the sea nitrogen and oxygen react to give poisonous nitric oxide
nitrogen is highly soluble in water
questions.solution: In blood, He is much less soluble than nitrogen, hence $\mathrm{He}-\mathrm{O}_2$ mixture is used by deep sea divers in preference to $\mathrm{N}_2-\mathrm{O}_2$ mixture.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 0.16 g of dibasic acid requires 25 mL decimolar NaOH solution for complete neutralisation. The molecular mass of the acid is
questions.solution:
0.16 g of dibasic acid is neutralised by $=25 \mathrm{~mL}$ of 0.1 M NaOH $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{X}+2 \mathrm{NaOH}=\mathrm{Na}_2 X+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Millimoles of acid } \begin{aligned} &=\frac{1}{2} \times \text { millimoles of } \mathrm{NaOH} \\ &=\frac{1}{2} \times 25 \times 0.1 \\ &=1.25 \\ & \because \quad \text { Millimoles }=\frac{\mathrm{W}(\mathrm{mg})}{\mathrm{mol} . \mathrm{mass}} \\ & \begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Mol. mass } & =\frac{\mathrm{W}(\mathrm{mg})}{\text { millimoles }} \\ & =\frac{0.16 \times 1000}{1.25} \\ & =128\end{aligned}\end{aligned} ⿱ 一 \begin{aligned} \because\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The number of $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ions in 100 mL of 0.001 M NaCl solution is
questions.solution:
100 mL of $0.001 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaCl}^{-2}$ contain $\mathrm{Cl}^{-1}$ ions
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{0.001}{1000} \times 100=10^{-4} \mathrm{~mol} \\ & =10^{-4} \times\left(6.022 \times 10^{23}\right)=6.022 \times 10^{19} \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \text {ions }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In a mixture of $1 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{H}_2$ and $8 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{O}_2$ the mole fraction of hydrogen is
questions.solution:
Number of moles of hydrogen $\left(\mathrm{H}_2\right)=\frac{\text { Weight in gram of } \mathrm{H}_2}{\text { Molecular weight of } \mathrm{H}_2}$
Weight of $\mathrm{H}_2$ in gram $=1 \mathrm{~g}$ molecular weight of $\mathrm{H}_2=2$ Number of moles of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{1}{2}=0.5 \mathrm{moles}$ Number of moles of $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{O}_2 & =\frac{\text { Weight in g of } \mathrm{O}_2}{\text { Molecular weight of } \mathrm{O}_2}=\frac{8}{32} \\ & =0.25 \text { moles } \end{aligned}$
Mole fraction of $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{H}_2 & =\frac{\text { Number of moles of } \mathrm{H}_2}{\text { Total number of moles }} \\ & =\frac{0.5}{0.25+0.5}=0.667 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: What mass of calcium chloride in grams would be enough to produce 14.35 g of $\mathrm{AgCl} ?($ Atomic mass $\mathrm{Ca}=40, \mathrm{Ag}=108)$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ required to produce $2 \times 143.5 \mathrm{~g}$ of AgCl $=111 \mathrm{~g}$ $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ required to produce 14.35 g of AgCl $=\frac{111 \times 14.35}{2 \times 143.5}=5.55 \mathrm{~g}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the reaction $\mathrm{N}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_3$, ratio by volume of $\mathrm{N}_2, \mathrm{H}_2$ and $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is $1: 3: 2$. This illustrates law of :
questions.solution: The volumes of the reacting gases and those of gaseous products bear the simple ratio. This is called the law of gaseous volumes or Gay-Lussac's law.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Arrange the following in increasing order of the volume (in L) occupied by them at STP
(i) 1.5 moles of $\mathrm{CO}_2$
(ii) 14 g of $\mathrm{N}_2$
(iii) $10^{21}$ molecules of oxygen

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A certain amount of a metal whose equivalent mass is 28 displaces $0.7 \mathrm{~L}^{-}$of $\mathrm{H}_2$ at STP from an acid. Hence, mass of the element is
questions.solution:
Mass of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{0.7}{22.4} \times 2=\frac{14}{224} \mathrm{~g}=0.0625 \mathrm{~g}$
$\therefore 0.0625 \mathrm{~g}$ of hydrogen is displaced by $x \mathrm{~g}$ of metal.
$\therefore 1 \mathrm{~g}$ of hydrogen is displaced by $\frac{x}{0.0625}$ g of metal.
$\therefore \quad \frac{x}{0.0625}=28$
$\therefore$ Mass of met $(x)=28 \times 0.0625=1.75 \mathrm{~g}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An organic compound $A$ contains $20 \% \mathrm{C}$, $46.66 \% \mathrm{~N}$ and $6.66 \% \mathrm{H}$. It gives $\mathrm{NH}_3$ gas on heating with $\mathrm{NaOH} . A$ can be
questions.solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
& C=\frac{20}{12}=1.66=1 \\
& N=\frac{46.66}{14}=3.33=2 \\
& H=\frac{6.66}{1}=6.66=4 \\
& O=\frac{26.68}{16}=1.66=1
\end{aligned}\)
Empirical formula of compound \(\mathrm{CN}_2 \mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{O}\)
Since, this organic compound contains carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen and oxygen and on heating with alkali liberates \(\mathrm{NH}_3\) the organic compound must be urea.
\(\mathrm{NH}_2 \mathrm{CONH}_2+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{NH}_3+\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3\)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A gaseous compounds having carbon, hydrogen and oxygen was burnt in the presence of oxygen. After combustion, 1 volume of the gaseous compound produces 2 volumes of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and 2 volumes of steam. Determines the molecular formula of the compound, it all the volumes were measured under the same conditions of pressure and temperature.
questions.solution:
Suppose the chemical formula of the compound be $\mathrm{C}_x \mathrm{H}_y \mathrm{O}_z$ chemical reaction related to combustion of the compound.

Since, 2 molecules of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ have 2 atoms of carbon and these carbon must have come from the compound. Hence, the value of $x$ is 2 . Again, 2 molecules of steam contain 4 atoms of hydrogen and they have come from the compound. Hence, the value of $y$ will be 4. Again, total oxygen atoms in the product side $=6$ But 4 oxygen atoms must have come from the oxygen molecules. Hence 2 oxygen atoms definitely come from the compound. Hence, the value of $z=2$. So, the molecular formula of the compound is $\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A silicon chip used in an integrated circuit of computer has a mass of 5.68 mg . The number of Si atoms present in this chip are
questions.solution:
Molar mass of silicon $=28 \mathrm{~g}=28000 \mathrm{mg}$
$\because 28000 \mathrm{mg}$ of silicon contain $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ atoms
$\therefore 5.68 \mathrm{mg}$ of silicon contain $\frac{6.022 \times 10^{23}}{28000} \times 5.68$
$\begin{aligned} & =0.00122 \times 10^{23} \\ & =1.22 \times 10^{20} \text { atoms }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Cost of sugar $\left(\mathrm{C}_{12} \mathrm{H}_{22} \mathrm{O}_{11}\right)$ is $₹ 50$ per kg. Calculate its cost per mol.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \text { Molar mass of sugar } & =12 \times 12+1 \times 22+11 \times 16 \\ & =144+22+176\end{aligned}$
$=342 \mathrm{~g}$
$\because$ Cost of 1000 g of sugar is $₹ 50$.
$\therefore$ Cost of 342 g of sugar $=\frac{50}{1000} \times 342$
$=17.1$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A man writes his biodata with carbon pencil on the plane paper having mass 150 mg . After writing his biodata, he weighs the written paper and find its mass is 152 mg . What is the number of carbon atoms present in the paper?
questions.solution:
Mass of $C$-atoms $=152-150=2 \mathrm{mg}$
Molar mass of C-atoms $=12 \mathrm{~g}=12000 \mathrm{mg}$
$\because 12000 \mathrm{mg}$ of carbon contains $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ atoms carbon
$\therefore 2 \mathrm{mg}$ of carbon contains $=\frac{6.022 \times 10^{23}}{12000} \times 2$
$\begin{aligned} & =0.0010036 \times 10^{23} \\ & =1.0036 \times 10^{20} \text { atoms }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: How many moles of $\mathrm{Al}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$ would be in 50 g of the substance?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An aqueous solution of phosphoric acid, $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ being titrated has molarity equal to 0.25 M . Which of the following could be normality of this solution?
questions.solution: As phosphoric acid is a tribasic acid, its $n$-factor can be 1, 2 or 3 . Hence, its normality can be $M$, $2 M$ or $3 M$, Where, $M$ is molarity of solution. Therefore, $N=0.25,0.50$ or 0.75 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
For the reaction represented by the equation,
$\mathrm{CX}_4+2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2+2 \mathrm{X}_2 \mathrm{O}$
9.0 g of $\mathrm{CX}_4$ completely reacts with 1.74 g of oxygen. The approximate molar mass of $X$ will be
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{CX}_4+2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2+2 \mathrm{X}_2 \mathrm{O} \\ & 1 \mathrm{~mol} \quad 2 \mathrm{~mol}=2 \times 32 \mathrm{~g} . \\ & 9.0 \mathrm{~g} \quad 1.74 \mathrm{~g} .\end{aligned}$
$\because 1.74 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{O}_2$ reacts with $\mathrm{CX}_4=9.0 \mathrm{~g}$
$\therefore 64 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{O}_2$ will react with $\mathrm{CX}_4=\frac{9.0 \times 64}{1.74}=331$
Molecular mass of $\mathrm{CX}_4=331$
or
$\begin{aligned}
331 & =12+4 X \\
X & =\frac{331-12}{4}=79.8 \approx 80
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The mass of $\mathrm{CaCO}_3$ required to react with 25 mL of 0.75 M HCl is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 1 g of fuming $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ ( oleum : It is a mixture of concentrated $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ saturated with $\mathrm{SO}_3$ and having formula $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ ) is diluted with $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$. This solution is completely neutralised by 26.7 mL of 0.8 N NaOH . Find the percentage of free $\mathrm{SO}_3$ in the oleum.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
$\mathrm{SO}_3$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$ is converted into $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$, hence $\mathrm{SO}_3$ acts also as a dibasic acid.
Eq. wt. $\left(\mathrm{SO}_3\right)=\frac{M}{2}=40$
Let $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ in fuming $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4=\mathrm{xg}$
$\mathrm{SO}_3=(1-x) \mathrm{g}$
Equiválent of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4=\frac{X}{49}$
$\mathrm{SO}_3=\frac{1-x}{40}$
Equivalent of NaOH used $=\frac{26.7 \times 0.8}{1000}$
$=0.02136$
$\frac{x}{49}+\frac{1-x}{40}=0.02136$
$x=0.7927 \mathrm{gH}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ in 1 g oleum.
Percentage of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4=79.27 \%$
$\mathrm{SO}_3=20.73 \%$

questions.subject: Chemistry
