questions.q: A sample of ammonium phosphate $\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_3 \mathrm{PO}_4$ contains 3.18 moles of H -atom. The number of moles of O -atom in the sample is
questions.solution:
Number of moles of O atoms $=\frac{1}{3}$ moles of H atoms
$=\frac{1}{3} \times 3.18=1.06 \mathrm{~mol}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A compound has the molecular formula $X_4 \mathrm{O}_6$. If 10 g of $X_4 \mathrm{O}_6$ has $5.72 \mathrm{~g} X$, then calculate atomic mass of $X$.
questions.solution:
Molecular weight of $X_4 \mathrm{O}_6=(4 a+96)$
$\begin{array}{r}
\because(4 a+96) \mathrm{g}_4 \mathrm{O}_6 \text { contains } X=4 a \mathrm{~g} \\
\therefore \quad 10 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{X}_4 \mathrm{O}_6 \text { contains } X=\frac{4 a \times 10}{4 a+96} \\
5.72=\frac{4 a \times 10}{4 a+96}
\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned}
22.88 a+549.12 & =40 a \\
17.12 a & =549.12 \\
a & =32.07 \mathrm{amu} .
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The largest number of molecules is in
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 36 \mathrm{~g} \text { of water }=\frac{36}{18}=2 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & 28 \mathrm{~g} \text { of } \mathrm{CO}_2=\frac{28}{44}=0.64 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & 46 \mathrm{~g} \text { of } \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}=\frac{46}{32}=1.44 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & 58 \mathrm{~g} \text { of } \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5=\frac{58}{108}=0.54 \mathrm{~mol}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: One mole of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ contains
questions.solution:
One mole $=6.02 \times 10^{23}$ molecules/atoms/ions
$\because \mathrm{CO}_2$ molecule has one C and two oxygen atoms.
$\therefore \mathrm{CO}_2$ contains $6.023 \times 10^{23}$ atoms of C and $6.023 \times 10^{23}$ molecules of $\mathrm{O}_2$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 100 mL of 1.0 M HCl are mixed with 75 mL of $1.0 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$. The resulting solution will be :
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3+2 \mathrm{HCl}-2 \mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{CO}_2$
Meq of $\mathrm{HCl}=1.0 \times 100$ Meq of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3=2 \times 1.0 \times 75=150$ Thus, $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ is in excess, and being the salt of weak acid $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3\right)$ and strong base $(\mathrm{NaOH})$, undergoes hydrolysis to produce basic character. $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Na}^{+}+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: What is the kinetic energy of 1 g of $\mathrm{O}_2$ at $47^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ?
questions.solution:
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{KE}=\frac{3}{2} R T(\text { for } 1 \mathrm{~mole})$
Given, $\quad T=47+273=320 \mathrm{~K}$
$\mathrm{KE}=\frac{3}{2} \times 8.314 \times 320=3990.7 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{O}_2=32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
$\begin{aligned} \text { Molar mass of } \mathrm{O}_2 & =32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \\ \mathrm{KE}\left(\text { per gram of } \mathrm{O}_2\right) & =\frac{3990.7}{32} \mathrm{Jg}^{-1} \\ & =124.7 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~g}^{-1} \\ & =1.24 \times 10^2 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~g}^{-1}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If $p, T, \rho$ and $R$ represents pressure, temperature, density and universal gas constant respectively, then the molar mass of the ideal gas is given by
questions.solution:
If $p, T, \rho$ and $R$ represents pressure, temperature, density and universal gas constant then ideal gas equation $p V=n R T$ for $n$ mole of gas $p V=R T$ for 1 mole of gas We know that $M=V \times \rho$ $V=\frac{M}{\rho}$
Put the value of $V$ in gas equation $p \times \frac{M}{\rho}=R T$ then molar mass of gas $M=\frac{\rho R T}{p}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In van der Waals' equation of gaseous state, the constant ' $b$ ' is a measure of
questions.solution:
van der Waals' equation for 1 mole of a real gas is $\left(p+\frac{a}{V^2}\right)(V-b)=R T$
where, $b$ is volume correction. It arises due to effective size of molecules.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is incorrect statement for liquid?
options: Surface tension of a liquid decreases with increase in temperature
Viscosity of a liquid decreases with decrease in temperature
Vapour pressure of a liquid increases with increase in temperature
Droplets of a liquid on flat surface are slightly flattened due to gravity
questions.solution: Viscosity of a liquid increases with decrease in temperature as kinetic energy of the molecules decreases and hence, intermolecular attraction increases which in turn increases the viscosity of a liquid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The ratio of rate of diffusion of $\mathrm{SO}_2(M=64)$ and oxygen $(M=32)$ is
questions.solution:
Molecular weight of $\mathrm{SO}_2\left(M_1\right)=64$
Molecular weight of $\mathrm{O}_2\left(\mathrm{M}_2\right)=32$ According to Graham's law of diffusion $\begin{aligned} \frac{r_1}{r_2} & =\sqrt{\frac{M_2}{M_1}} \\ \frac{r_1}{r_2} & =\sqrt{\frac{32}{64}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\ & =\frac{1}{1.414} \\ r_1: r_2 & =1: 1.414 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following is the weakest acid?
questions.solution: HF has the highest bond dissociation enthalpy amongst all the halogen acids. The hydration enthalpy of the fluoride ion is also highest. The former predominates making HF the weakest of the halogen acids.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: 1 mol He and $3 \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~N}_2$ exert a pressure of 16 atm . Due to a hole in the vessel in which mixture in placed, mixture leaks out. What is the composition of mixture effusing out initially?
questions.solution:
$\frac{r_1}{r_2}=\frac{p_1}{p_2} \sqrt{\frac{M_2}{M_1}}$
$\begin{aligned} & P_{\mathrm{He}}=x_{\mathrm{He}} \cdot P_{\text {total }} \\ &=\frac{1}{4} \times 16=4 \text { atom }\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P_{N_2}=x_{N_2} \cdot p_{\text {total }} \\ &=\frac{3}{4} \times 16=12 \text { atom }=\left(p_{\text {total }}^{-} p_{\text {He }}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \frac{r_{\mathrm{He}}}{r_{\mathrm{N}_2}} & =\frac{p_{\mathrm{He}}}{p_{\mathrm{N}_2}} \sqrt{\frac{M\left(\mathrm{~N}_2\right)}{M(\mathrm{He})}} \\ & =\frac{4}{12} \sqrt{\frac{28}{4}}=0.88\end{aligned}$
Hence, moles of He and $\mathrm{N}_2$ effusing out initially are in the ratio $0.88: 1$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The ratio of rate of diffusion of hydrogen and helium gas is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \because \quad \frac{r_{\mathrm{H}}}{r_{\mathrm{He}}} & =\sqrt{\frac{M_{\mathrm{He}}}{M_{\mathrm{H}}}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{4}{2}}=\sqrt{2}=1.4 \\ \therefore r_{\mathrm{H}}: r_{\mathrm{He}} & =1.4: 1\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The root mean square velocity of a gas is doubled when the temperature is
questions.solution:
$v_{\mathrm{rms}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{M}}$
$\therefore v_{\mathrm{rms}} \propto \sqrt{T}$
$\therefore$ At two different temperatures,
$\frac{v_{\mathrm{rms}}}{v_{\mathrm{rms}}^{\prime}}=\sqrt{\frac{T}{T^{\prime}}}$
Given,$v_{\mathrm{rms}}^{\prime}=2 v_{\mathrm{rms}}$
$\frac{1}{2}=\sqrt{\frac{T}{T^{\prime}}}$
or$\frac{1}{4}=\frac{T}{T^{\prime}}$
$\therefore  T^{\prime}=4 T$
$\therefore v_{\mathrm{rms}}$ gets doubled, when the temperature is increased four times.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Deviation from the ideal gas behaviour of a gas can be expressed as
questions.solution:
Deviation from ideal gas behaviour of a gas is expressed in terms of compressibility factor, $Z$
$Z=\frac{p V}{n R T}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Under which of the following condition, vander waals' gas approaches ideal behaviour?
questions.solution: Van der Waals' gas approaches ideal behaviour at extremely low pressure and high temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The kinetic energy of a gas molecule is _____ temperature
options: directly proportional to square root of
questions.solution:
The kinetic energy of a gas molecule is directly proportional to absolute temperature. According to kinetic theory of gases, the pressure $P$ exerted by a gas is given by $p=\frac{1}{3} \frac{M u^2}{V}$ or $p V=\frac{1}{3} M u^2$
According to gas equation $p V=R T$ $\therefore \quad \frac{1}{3} M u^2=R T$ or $\quad \frac{1 M u^2}{3 N}=\frac{R T}{N}$ We know that $\frac{R}{N}=K$ $K=$ Boltzmann constant or $\quad \frac{1}{2} M u^2=\frac{3}{2} K T$ But $\frac{1}{2} M u^2$ is average kinetic energy per molecule of the gas. $\therefore \quad$ Average K.E. $=\frac{3}{2} K T$ K.E. $\propto T$ Thus average kinetic energy of one mole of any gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: At which temperature nitrogen under 1.00 atm pressure has the same root mean square speed as that of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ at STP
questions.solution:
Let RMS speed of nitrogen at $T \mathrm{~K}$ be $u$ and is equal to that of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ at STP
$\begin{aligned} u & =\sqrt{\frac{3 R T}{28}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 R \times 273}{44}} \\ T & =\frac{273 \times 28}{44}=173.73 \mathrm{~K}=-99.27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The order of increasing energies of the orbitals follows:
questions.solution:
According to aufbau principle. The order of increasing energies may be summed up as below. $1 s, 2 s, 2 p, 3 s, 3 p, 4 s, 3 d, 4 p .5 s, 4 d, 5 p, 6 s$, $4 f, 5 d, 6 p, 7 s, 5 f, 6 d$ and $7 p$
So, the correct order is $5 p \lt 6 s \lt 4 f \lt 5 d$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following conclusion is correct regarding photoelectric effect?
options: Energy of a photon depends upon frequency of light absorbed.
Energy of a photon depends upon intensity of light.
Kinetic energy of electrons increases due to increase in intensity of light.
All the energy of photon is absorbed by metal súrface.
questions.solution: Energy of photon depends upon frequency and not on intensity. As on increasing the intensity of light of a particular frequency $v$, the number of electrons ejected increases but not their kinetic energy.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The wave number of the shortest wavelength of absorption spectrum of hydrogen atom is _____ (Rydberg constant $\left.=109700 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}\right)$.
questions.solution:
Given, $R=109700 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$
Shortest wavelength mean transition is from infinity to $n=1$.
Using the formula,
$\begin{aligned}
& \bar{v}=R\left[\frac{1}{n_f^2}-\frac{1}{n_i^2}\right] \\
& n_t=\infty, n_i=n=1 \\
& \bar{v}=109700\left[\frac{1}{1}-\frac{1}{\infty}\right] \\
& \bar{v}=109700 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
What is the wavelength associated with an electron moving with a velocity of $10^6 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ ?
$\left(h=6.63 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}\right)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \lambda & =\frac{h}{m v}=\frac{6.63 \times 10^{-34}}{9.11 \times 10^{-31} \times 10^6} \\ & =0.727 \times 10^{-9}=0.727 \mathrm{~nm}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: For which of the following species Bohr's theory is not applicable?
questions.solution: Bohr's atom model is applicable only to one electron splcies, $\mathrm{He}^{2+}$ does not contain any electron all other species have one electron each.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following ions has the highest magnetic moment?
questions.solution:
Magnetic moment $\propto$ no. of unpaired electron.
$\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}=3 d^{10} 4 s^0$ unpaired electron $=0 ;$
$\mu=0$
$\mathrm{T} \mathrm{I}^{3+}=3 d^{10} 4 s^0$ unpaired electron=1
$\mu=\sqrt{n(n+2)}=1.73$
$\mathrm{Sc}^{3+}=3 d^0 4 s^0$ unpaired electron $=0 ;$
$\mu=0$
$\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}=3 d^5 4 s^0$ unpaired electrons $=5$
$\mu=\sqrt{5(5+2)}=5.91$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $n / p$ ratio during positron decay
questions.solution: A decay process is possible only if accompanied by the release of energy. There are three different decay processes that increase the $n / p$ ratio. These are : (i) electron capture, (ii) position emission ( $\beta^{+}$- emission) and (iii) alpha particle decay.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following notations shows the product incorrectly?
questions.solution:
${ }_7^{13} \mathrm{~N} \xrightarrow{-{ }_2 \mathrm{He}^{+}}{ }_5^{10} \mathrm{~B}$
${ }_{96}^{242} \mathrm{Cm} \xrightarrow{-{ }_0 \mathrm{n}^1}{ }_{94}^{244} \mathrm{Pu}$
${ }_7^{14} \mathrm{~N} \xrightarrow{-{ }^{\mathrm{n}^1}}{ }_6^{14} \mathrm{C}$
${ }_{15}^{30} \mathrm{P} \xrightarrow[-\mathrm{g}]{-\mathrm{H}^1}{ }_{14}^{28} \mathrm{Si}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The magnetic quantum number for $d$-orbital is given by
questions.solution:
Azimuthal quantum number $l$ for $d$ orbitals is 2 . so the value of magnetic quantum number is
$-l \text { to }+l$
then
$-2 \text { to }+2-2,-1,0,+1,+2$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In the atomic spectrum of hydrogen, the spectral lines pertaining to electronic transition of $n=4$ to $n=2$ refers to
questions.solution:
In Balmer series electrons transfer from higher orbits to $2^{\text {nd }}$ orbit.
$\begin{aligned}
& n_2=3,4,5,6, \ldots \\
& n_1=2
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following ions has the highest magnetic moment?
questions.solution:
$\mu=\sqrt{n(n+2)}$
where, $n=$ no. of unpaired electrons. Hence, the ion which has the maximum number of unpaired electron, will show the highest magnetic moment. $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ has maximum 5 unpaired electrons, hence it shows the highest magnetic moment.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following sets of quantum numbers is impossible arrangement?
questions.solution: For the value $n=3$, the value of $l$ are 0,1 and 2 . Hence, $m$ cannot have value 3 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: For $f$-orbital the values of $m$ are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In hydrogen spectrum, the series of lines appearing in ultra violet region of electromagnetic spectrum are called
questions.solution: In the hydrogen spectrum the Lyman series of lines appears in ultra violet region of electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If uncertainty in position and velocity are equal, then uncertainty in momentum will be
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \because x & =\Delta v \\ \Delta x \times \Delta p & \geq \frac{h}{4 \pi} \\ \Delta x \times m \cdot \Delta v & =\frac{h}{4 \pi} \\ (\Delta v)^2 & =\frac{h}{4 \pi m} \quad(\because \Delta x=\Delta v) \\ \Delta p & =m \cdot \Delta v \\ & =m \sqrt{\frac{h}{4 \pi m}}=\sqrt{\frac{m h}{4 \pi}} \\ \Delta p & =\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{\frac{m h}{\pi}}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: For azimuthal quantum number $l=3, m$ can have
questions.solution:
There are $(2 l+1)$ values of $m$ for the each value of $l$.
$\therefore l=3$
$\therefore$ Total values of $m=(2 \times 3+1)=7$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The energy of second Bohr orbit of the hydrogen atom is $-328 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$, hence the energy of fourth Bohr orbit would be
questions.solution:
Energy of electron in $n$th orbit of hydrogen,
$E_n=-\frac{E}{n^2}$
where, $E$ is a constant.
$\Rightarrow \frac{E_2}{E_4}=\frac{\left(n_4\right)^2}{\left(n_2\right)^2}$
$\frac{-328}{E_4}=\frac{(4)^2}{(2)^2}$
$\therefore E_4=\frac{-328 \times 4}{16}=-82 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Calculate the energy of photons having wavelength, $5 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~m}$ falls on a metal surface of work function, $3.4 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~J}$.
questions.solution:
Energy of photons,
$E=h \nu=\frac{h c}{\lambda}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{\left(6.626 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{Js}\right) \times\left(3 \times 10^8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}\right)}{5 \times 10^{-7}} \\ & =3.975 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Select the correct options according to Hund's rule and Pauli exclusion principle.
Codes
questions.solution:
According to Pauli exclusion principle, no two electron in an atom can have the same set of four quantum numbers.
Hund's rule states that the pairing of electrons in the orbital of a particular sub-shell $(p, d$ or $f$ ) does not take place until all the orbitals of the subshell are singly filled.
Option I and II do not follow Hund's rule and Paulis exclusion principle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The Pauli exclusion principle applies to the following option.
questions.solution: Since, $\mathrm{He}^{+}$and H have one electron each, while $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ has no electron, hence Pauli exclusion principle does not apply to them. However, $\mathrm{H}^{-}$has two electrons, hence this principle applies on it.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following set of quantum number is not applicable for an electron in an atom?
questions.solution:
For a given $n$ value possible l values are 0 to $(n-1)$ possible $m$ values for a given l value is
$-l, 0,+I$ and value of $s$ for an electron $=+\frac{1}{2}$ or $-\frac{1}{2}$. So for $n=1, I=0$ not 1 and $m=0$ not 1 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is not possible ?
questions.solution:
When $n=3, l=0,1,2$ and
For
$\begin{aligned} & l=0, m=0 \\ & l=1, m=-1,0,+1 \\ & l=2, m=-2,-1,0,+1,+2\end{aligned}$
So, for the value $l=0, m$ cannot be -1

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The ionisation energy of hydrogen atom is 13.6 eV . What will be the ionisation energy of $\mathrm{He}^{+}$?
questions.solution:
Ionisation energy of $\mathrm{He}^{+}=13.6 \times Z^2 \mathrm{eV}$
$\begin{aligned} & =13.6 \times(2)^2 \mathrm{eV} \\ & =13.6 \times 4 \mathrm{eV}=54.4 \mathrm{eV}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The isoelectronic pair is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Cl}_2 \mathrm{O}=2 \times 17+16=42 \text { electrons } \\ & \mathrm{ICl}_2^{-}=53+2 \times 17+1=88 \text { electrons } \\ & \mathrm{Cl}_2^{-}=2 \times 17+1=35 \text { electrons }\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{IF}_2^{+}=53+2 \times 9-1=70 \text { electrons } \\
& \mathrm{ClO}_2=17+2 \times 8=33 \text { electrons } \\
& \mathrm{ClO}_2=17+2 \times 8+1=33 \text { electrons } \\
& \mathrm{ClO}_2^{-}=17+2 \times 8+1=34 \text { electrons } \\
& \mathrm{Cl}_2^{+}=17+2 \times 9-1=34 \text { electrons }
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{ClO}_2^{-}$and $\mathrm{ClF}_2^{+}$contain 34 electrons each.
Hence they are isoelectronic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The ground state term symbol for an electronic state is governed by
questions.solution:
In the ground state of an atom, the number of states is limited by Hund's rule. These are $\frac{L n}{L r .(n-r)!}$ ways in which electron in orbital may be arranged which do not violate Paulis exclusion principle.
Where, $n=$ number of maximum electrons that can be filled in an orbital
and $r=$ number of electrons present in orbital.
But the valid ground state term is calculated by Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity. As Hund's rule gives the most stable electronic configuration of electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An atomic orbital has two angular nodes and one radial node. It is a
questions.solution: Two angular nodes are found in $d$-orbitals. Also for a $d$-orbital to have one radial node it must be 4d. $[\because$ Number of radial nodes $=n-I-1]$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Valence electrons in the element $A$ are 3 and that in element $B$ are 6. Most probable compound formed from $A$ and $B$ is
questions.solution:
Valence electron in $A=3$
Valence electron in $B=6$
Thus, $A$ is electropositive and $B$ is electronegative. A can lose three electrons and $B$ can gain two electrons to attain stable configuration.
Thus, $A$ exists as $A^{3+}$ and $B$ as $B^{2-}$ thus, compound is $A_2 B_3$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is not true in Rutherford's nuclear' model of atom?
options: Protons and neutrons are present inside nucleus
Volume of nucleus is very small as compared to volume of atom
The number of protons and neutrons are always equal
The number of electrs and protons are always equal
questions.solution: The sum of protons and neutrons give the mass number. The number of neutrons and protons are not equal.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: What are the four quantum numbers of 19 th electron in $\mathrm{Sc}(Z=21)$ ?
questions.solution:
Sc $(21)=1 s^2 2 s^2 2 p^6 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^1 s^2$
19th electron goes into 4 s
Hence, $n=4$   $l=0$,
$m=0, \quad m_s=+\frac{1}{2}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: ${ }_7^{14} \mathrm{~N}+{ }_0^1 n \rightarrow{ }_6^{14} \mathrm{C}+{ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}$ is written as
questions.solution:
${ }_7^{14} \mathrm{~N}+{ }_0^1 n \longrightarrow{ }_6^{14} \mathrm{C}+{ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}$
This reaction can be represented as
${ }_7^{14} \mathrm{~N}(n, p){ }_6^{14} \mathrm{C}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following species have the same number of electrons in its outermost as well as penultimate shell?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{Cl}^{-} & =2,8,8 \\
\mathrm{O}^{2-} & =2,8 \\
\mathrm{Na}^{+} & =2,8 \\
\mathrm{Mg}^{2+} & =2,8
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$contains same number of electrons in outermost and penultimate shells.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The triad of the nuclei that is isotonic, is
questions.solution:
Isotonic species have the same the number of neutrons. No. of neutrons in $\begin{aligned} & { }_6 \mathrm{C}^{14}=14-6=8 \\ & { }_7 \mathrm{~N}^{14}=14-7=7 \\ & { }_9 \mathrm{~F}^{19}=19-9=10 \\ & { }_7 \mathrm{~N}^{15}=15-7=8 \\ & { }_9 \mathrm{~F}^{17}=15-7=8 \\ & { }_6 \mathrm{C}^{12}=12-6=6 \end{aligned}$
Hence, ${ }_6 \mathrm{C}^{14},{ }_7 \mathrm{~N}^{15},{ }_9 \mathrm{~F}^{17}$ are isotonic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following has magic number of neutrons?
questions.solution:
Magic numbers are $2,8,20,28,50$ or 82 protons or $2,8,20,28,50,82$ or 126 neutrons in the nucleus. These numbers impart stability to the nucleus.
(a) ${ }_{13} \mathrm{Al}^{27}$, no. of neutrons $=27-13=14$
(b) ${ }_{26} \mathrm{Fe}^{56}$, no. of neutrons $=56-26=30$
(c) ${ }_{83} \mathrm{Bi}^{209}$, no. of neutrons $=209-83=126$
(d) $92 \mathrm{U}^{238}$, no. of neutrons $=238-92=146$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: For the valence electron in copper, the four quantum numbers are
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
{ }_{29} \mathrm{Cu} & =2,8,18,1 \\
& =1 s^2, 2 s^2 2 p^6, 3 s^2 3 p^6 3 d^{10}, 4 s^1
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Quantum numbers for $4 s^1$ electron are
$n=4, \quad l=0, \quad m=0, s=+\frac{1}{2}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: What is the magnetic moment of $\left[\mathrm{FeF}_6\right]^{3-}$ ?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Orbital is
options: circular path around the nucleus in which electrons are revolves
space around the nucleus where the probability of finding the electron is maximum
questions.solution: An atomic orbital is a three dimensional region of definite shape around the nucleus where the probability of finding the electron is maximum. Therefore, an atom has both characteristic energy and a characteristic shape.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following sets of quantum number is not permitted?
questions.solution: For $n, l=0$ to $(n-1)$ Therefore, when $n=3, l \neq 3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: An element $M$ has an atomic mass of 19 and atomic number 9 , its ions is represented by
questions.solution: Configuration of an element ${ }_9 M=1 s^2, 2 s^2, 2 p^5$. Hence, its ion will be $\mathrm{M}^{-}$because it can easily gain one electron to have octet.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following transitions involves maximum amount of energy?
questions.solution: $M^{2+} \longrightarrow M^{3+}$, after the removal of $2 e^{-}$, the nuclear charge per electron increases, due to which high energy is required to remove $3 e^{-}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In which one of the following pairs the radius of the second species is greater than that of the first?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{O}^{2-}$ and $\mathrm{N}^{3-}$ both are isoelectronic but differ in the charge possessed by them. As the negative charge increases, the electrons are held less and less tightly by the nucleus, therefore ionic radii increases. Hence, ionic radii of $\mathrm{N}^{3-}$ is greater than $\mathrm{O}^{2-}$.
In a period from left to right atomic radii decreases but in a group on moving downwards it increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is $0.529 Å$. The radius of the third orbit of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$will be
questions.solution:
According to Bohr model, radius of hydrogen atom
$\left(r_n\right)=\frac{0.529 \times n^2}{Z} Å$
(where, $n=$ number of orbit, $Z=$ atomic number)
$r_3=\frac{0.529 \times(3)^2}{1}=4.761 Å$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following element has maximum photoelectric effect?
questions.solution:
The phenomenon of emission of electrons from the surface of metal by visible light is called photoelectric effect.
All alkali metals (except lithium) exhibits photoelectric effect. This ability to exhibit photoelectric effect is due to low value of their ionisation energies. Among alkali metals, Cs having least ionisation energy, exhibits maximum photoelectric effect.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: ${ }_{88}^{228} X-3 \alpha,-\beta \longrightarrow Y$. The element $Y$ is
questions.solution: ${ }_{88}^{228} X \xrightarrow{-3 \alpha}{ }_{82}^{216} \mathrm{~Pb} \xrightarrow{-\beta}{ }_{83}^{216} \mathrm{Bi}(Y)$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: At 298 K the entropy of rhombic sulphur is $32.04 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol} \mathrm{K}$ and that of monoclinic sulphur is $32.68 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{mol} \mathrm{K}$. The heat of their combustion are respectively- 298246 and $-297948 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. $\Delta G$ for the reaction; $S_{\text {rhombic }} \longrightarrow S_{\text {monoclinic }}$ will be
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & S_{\text {thombic }} \longrightarrow S_{\text {monoclinic }} \\ & \Delta H=-297948-(-298246)=298 \mathrm{~J} \\ & \Delta S=32.68-32.04=0.64 \\ & \Delta G=\Delta H-T \Delta S=298-298 \times 0.64=107.28 \mathrm{~J}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The ratio of de-Broglie wavelengths for electron accelerated through 200 V and 50 V is
questions.solution:
$\lambda=\frac{h}{m v}=\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m \times(\mathrm{KE})}}$
$\begin{aligned} \lambda_1 & =\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m \times 200}} \\ \lambda_2 & =\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m \times 50}} \\ \frac{\lambda_1}{\lambda_2} & =\frac{1 \times \sqrt{50}}{\sqrt{200 \times 1}}=\frac{1}{2} \\ \lambda_1: \lambda_2 & =1: 2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following forms cationic micelles above a certain minimum concentration?
questions.solution: Cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide forms cationic micelles above a certain minimum concentration.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: During the adsorption of krypton on activated charcoal at low temperature.
questions.solution:
Adsorption is an exothermic process thus, $\Delta H$ is negative (i.e. $\Delta H \lt 0$ ). Moreover, adsorption results in more ordered arrangements of molecules, thus entropy decreases (i.e. $\Delta S \lt 0$ ).
Hence, low temperature favours the reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Four different colloids have the following gold number, which one has the most effective action?
questions.solution:
Protective power of a lyophilic colloid is measured in terms of gold number. Lower the value of gold number, better is the protective power of a protective colloid. Thus, the order will be
$\text { (a) }\gt\text { (b) }\gt\text { (d) }\gt\text { (c) }$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Multimolecular colloids are present in
questions.solution: In multimolecular colloids, colloidal size particles or molecules are built by the aggregation of ions or smaller particles. eg. gold sol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In physical adsorption, the adsorbent and adsorbate are held together by the
questions.solution: In physical adsorption, the adsorbent and adsorbate are held together by the van der Waals' forces.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: According to the adsorption theory of catalysis, the speed of reaction increases because
options: adsorption lowers the activation enegry of the reaction
the concentration of reactant molecules at the active centres of the catalyst becomes high due to adsorption
in the process of adsorption, the activation energy of the molecules becomes large
adsorption produces heat which increases the speed of the reaction
questions.solution: In heterogeneous catalysis, adsorption lowers the activation energy of the reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following electrolytes is least effective in coagulating ferric hydroxide solution?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ is a positively charged sol, thus coagulated by negative ion (anion). Smaller the charge on anion, smaller is its coagulating power or higher is its flocculation value.
(a) $\mathrm{KBr} \longrightarrow \mathrm{K}^{+}+\mathrm{Br}^{-}$
(b) $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}+\mathrm{SO}_4{ }^{2-}$
(c) $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CrO}_4 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}+\mathrm{CrO}_4{ }^{2-}$
(d) $\mathrm{K}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right] \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{~K}^{+}+\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{4-}$
$\because \mathrm{Br}^{-}$has smaller charge.
$\therefore \mathrm{KBr}$ is least effective in coagulating $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ sol.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Lyophilic sols are more stable than lyophobic sols because
options: the colloidal particles have negative charge
the colloidal particles have positive charge
questions.solution: Lyophilic sols are more stable than lyophobic sols because the colloidal particles are solvated.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following reaction requires catalyst?
questions.solution:
Reaction (a) and (b) are exothermic and spontaneous so do not need catalyst. On the . other hand, conversion of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ into $\mathrm{SO}_3$ need catalyst. Catalyst used for this reaction may be Pt or $\mathrm{V}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ (contact process) or NO (lead chamber process)
$2 \mathrm{SO}_2+\mathrm{O}_2 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{~V}_2 \mathrm{O}_5} 2 \mathrm{SO}_3$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Potassium stearate is obtained by the saponification of an oil or fat. It has formula $\mathrm{CH}_3-\left(\mathrm{CH}_2\right)_{16}-\mathrm{COOK}$. The molecule has a lyophobic terminal $\mathrm{CH}_3$ and a lyophilic terminal - COOK. Potassium stearate is an example of
questions.solution: Potassium stearate is an example of micelle.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Fog is a colloidal solution of
questions.solution: Fog is a liquid in gas colloidal solution.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In aerosol, the dispersion medium is
questions.solution: Aerosol is colloidal system of solid in gas, e.g, smoke. So, dispersion medium in aerosol is gas.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In physical adsorption, the gas molecules are held by solid surfaces through
questions.solution: Physical adsorption involves physical forces i.e., van der Waals' forces.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The maximum work obtained from a reversible process is given as
questions.solution: Decrease in the value of Gibbs energy $(-\Delta G)$ gives the maximum useful work or net work done by the system for the given change whereas decrease in Helmholtz free energy or work function $(-\Delta A)$ gives the maximum work done for the given change.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: If gas absorbs 150 J of heat and expands by $450 \mathrm{~cm}^3$ against a constant pressure of $2 \times 10^5 \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$, then change in internal energy is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Calculate the temperature at which $\Delta G=-5.2 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, \Delta H=145.6 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $\Delta S=216 \mathrm{Jk}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ for a chemical reaction :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \Delta G_1 & =\Delta H-T \Delta S \text { or } T=\frac{\Delta H-\Delta G}{\Delta S} \\ & =\frac{[145.6-(-5.2)] \times 10^3}{216}=698.1 \mathrm{~K}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \quad \Delta H=-94 \mathrm{kcal} \\ & 2 \mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \Delta H=-135.2 \mathrm{kcal}\end{aligned}$
The heat of formation of $\mathrm{CO}(\mathrm{g})$ is :
questions.solution:
The required reaction is $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{s})+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(g) & \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}(g) \\ \mathrm{CO}_2(g) & \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}(g)+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2(g)\end{aligned}$ $\Delta H=\frac{+135.2}{2} \mathrm{kcal}$...(i) $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) & \longrightarrow \mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})\end{aligned}$ $\Delta H =-94 \mathrm{kcal}$...(ii)
The required equation is come out by adding Eq. (i) and (ii) So, $\Delta H_{f(\mathrm{CO})}=-94+\frac{135.2}{2}=-26.4 \mathrm{kcal}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: In which of the following $\Delta E=\Delta H$ ?
questions.solution: $\Delta H=\Delta E+\Delta n_g R T$ where $\Delta n_g=$ number of moles of gaseous product - number of moles of gaseous reactant

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: For a process to be spontaneous
questions.solution: For a spontaneous process the value of $\Delta G$ must be negative.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The entropy change for the fusion of 1 mol of a solid which melts at $27^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is (latent heat of fusion $=600 \mathrm{cal} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ )
questions.solution:
$\Rightarrow \Delta S_{\text {fus }}=\frac{\Delta H_{\text {fus }}}{T}$
Given, $\begin{aligned} T & =27+273 \\ & =300 \mathrm{~K} \\ \therefore \quad \Delta S_{\text {fus }} & =\frac{600}{300}=2 \mathrm{cal} \mathrm{deg}^{-1}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The enthalpy change for the transition of liquid water to steam is $40.8 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ at 373 K . Calculate the entropy of vaporisation of water.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { } \Delta S_{\text {vap }}=\frac{\Delta H_{\text {vap }}}{T} \\ & \because \Delta H_{\text {vap }}=40.8 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, T=373 \mathrm{~K} \\ & \Delta S_{\text {vap }}=\frac{40.8 \times 10^3}{373}=109.4 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The standard free energy change $\left(\Delta G^{\circ}\right)$ is related to equilibrium constant $(K)$ as
questions.solution:
The relationship between the standard free energy $\left(\Delta G^{\circ}\right)$ and equilibrium constant $(K)$ is as
$\Delta G^{\circ}=-2.303 R T \log \mathrm{~K}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The equilibrium constant for a reaction is 10 $\Delta G^{\circ}$ will be $\left(R=8 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, T=300 \mathrm{~K}\right)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \Delta & G^{\circ}=-R T \ln K \\ \Delta G^{\circ} & =-2.303 R T \log K \\ & =-2.303 \times 8 \times 300 \times \log 10 \\ & =-2.303 \times 8 \times 300 \times 1 \\ & =-5527 \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}=-5.527 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: $C_p / C_V$ for noble gases is
questions.solution: Noble gases are monoatomic. The value of $C_P / C_V$ for noble gases is 1.66 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Enthalpy of formation of HF and HCl are -161 kJ and -92 kJ , respectively. Select the incorrect statement on the basis of the above fact.
options: HF is more stable than HCl
HCl is more stable than HF
Affinity of fluorine to hydrogen is greater than affinity of chlorine to hydrogen
HF and HCl are exothermic compound
questions.solution: HF is more stable than HCl because more energy is produced in the formation of HF. It means HF has less energy than HCl and hence is more stable. So, we need more energy to break H-F bond. Hence, HF is more stable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A reaction is spontaneous at low temperature, but non-spontaneous at high temperature. Which of the following condition is true for the chemical reaction?
questions.solution:
$\Delta H \lt 0, \Delta S \lt 0$
$\Delta G=\Delta H-T \Delta S$
When, $\Delta H \lt 0$ and $\Delta S \lt 0$, then $\Delta G$ will be negative only at low temperature.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The enthalpy of combustion of carbon and carbon monoxide are -393.5 and $-283 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$ respectively. The enthalpy of formation of carbon monoxide per mole is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Enthalpy is equal to
questions.solution:
The Gibbs-Helmholtz equation is as
$G=H+T\left[\frac{\delta G}{\delta T}\right]_p$
Dividing above equation by $T^2$
$\frac{G}{T^2}=\frac{H}{T^2}+\frac{1}{T}\left[\frac{\delta G}{\delta T}\right]_p$
This on rearrangement becomes.
$\left[\frac{\delta(G / T)}{\delta T}\right]=-\frac{H}{T^2}$
Thus, enthalpy, $H=-T^2\left[\frac{\delta G / T}{\delta T}\right]_p$
Where, $\quad H=$ enthalpy

A substance is carried out through the following transformations
The equation $\Delta S_1+\Delta S_2=\Delta S_3+\Delta S_4+\Delta S_5$

is always true because entropy is a state function
may be true but need more informations on the processes

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Following method of extracting Zn is based on thermodynamics
$A: 2 \mathrm{ZnS}+3 \mathrm{O}_3 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{ZnO}+2 \mathrm{SO}_2$
$B: \mathrm{ZnO}+\mathrm{C} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Zn}+\mathrm{CO}$
If $\Delta G_f^{\circ}$ (standard free energies of formation, in $\mathrm{kJ} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ ) of $\mathrm{ZnS}=-205.4$,
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{ZnO}=-318.0, \mathrm{SO}_2=-300.4 \\ & \mathrm{CO}=-137.3\end{aligned}$
Free energy changes of the above reaction $A$ and $B$ (respectively) will be
questions.solution:
For reaction $A$
$\begin{aligned} \Delta G^{\circ} & =2 \Delta G_f^{\circ}(\mathrm{ZnO})+2 \Delta G_f^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{SO}_2\right)-2 G_f^{\circ}(\mathrm{ZnS}) \\ & =2[-3182-300.4+205.4] \\ & =-826.4 \mathrm{~kJ} \quad\left(\Delta G_f^{\circ}(\text { element })=0\right)\end{aligned}$
For reaction $B$
$\begin{aligned} \Delta G^{\circ} & =\Delta G_f^{\circ}(\mathrm{CO})-\Delta G_f^{\circ}(\mathrm{ZnO}) \\ & =-137.3+328.2 \\ & =+180.9 \mathrm{~kJ}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following reaction energy diagrams best represents a reaction in the reverse direction; that it is the most endothermic?
questions.solution: In reverse direction, product becomes reactant and vice versa. Hence, (b) is most endothermic in reverse direction

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{S}_{\text {(rhombic) }}+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \\
\Delta H=-297.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \\
\mathrm{~S}_{\text {(monoclinic) }}+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{SO}_2(\mathrm{~g}), \\
\Delta H=-300 \mathrm{~kJ}
\end{aligned}$
The data can predict that
options: rhombic sulphur is yellow in colour
questions.solution:
Substracting equation (II) from (I), we get
$\mathrm{S}_R \longrightarrow \mathrm{~S}_M, \quad \Delta H=+2.5 \mathrm{~kJ}$
Thus, $\mathrm{S}_R$ to $\mathrm{S}_M$ transition is an endothermic reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A coffee cup calorimeter initially contains 125 g of water at a temperature of $24.2^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. After adding of 10.5 g of KBr , the temperature becomes $21.1^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The heat of solution is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \Delta H & =m s \Delta T=(125+10.5) \times 1 \times 3.1 \\ & =420.05 \mathrm{cal}=\frac{420.05}{10.5} \mathrm{cal} / \mathrm{g} \\ & =40 \mathrm{cal} / \mathrm{g}=167.7 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{g}\end{aligned}$
