questions.solution:
Here, $f(x)=\sqrt{\sin ^{-1}(2 x)+\frac{\pi}{6}}$, to find domain, we must have,
$\begin{array}{cc} 
& \sin ^{-1}(2 x)+\frac{\pi}{6} \geq 0 \quad\left(\text { but }-\frac{\pi}{2} \leq \sin ^{-1} \theta \leq \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\
\therefore & -\frac{\pi}{6} \leq \sin ^{-1}(2 x) \leq \sin \frac{\pi}{2} \\
\Rightarrow & \sin \left(-\frac{\pi}{6}\right) \leq 2 x \leq \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\
\Rightarrow & -\frac{1}{2} \leq 2 x \leq 1 \\
\Rightarrow & -\frac{1}{4} \leq x \leq \frac{1}{2} \\
\Rightarrow & x \in\left[-\frac{1}{4}, \frac{1}{2}\right]
\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
The period of
$f(x)=\sin \left(\frac{\pi x}{n-1}\right)+\cos \left(\frac{\pi x}{n}\right), n \in Z, n\gt2$
is
questions.solution:
Given that, $f(x)=\sin \left(\frac{\pi x}{n-1}\right)+\cos \left(\frac{\pi x}{n}\right)$
Let $f(x)=g(x)+p(x), \forall n\gt2$ $\therefore$ Period of $g(x)=\frac{2 \pi \times(n-1)}{\pi}=2(n-1)$ and period of $p(x)=\frac{2 \pi n}{\pi}=2 n$ Period of $f(x)=$ LCM of $p(x)$ and $q(x)$ $=2 n(n-1)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $f(x)=\cos \left[\pi^2\right] x+\cos \left[-\pi^2\right] x$, then
questions.solution:
We have, $f(x)=\cos \left[\pi^2\right] x+\cos \left[-\pi^2\right] x \ldots$ (i) Put the value of $\pi=3.141$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \pi^2=\text { app. } 9.6 \text { in (i) } \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)=\cos 9 x+\cos 10 x \quad \quad \quad[\because \pi=3.141] \\ & \text { Put } \quad \quad x=\frac{\pi}{4} \\ & f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\cos \frac{9 \pi}{4}+\cos \frac{10 \pi}{4}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+0=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \end{aligned}$
Now, $f(-\pi)=\cos 9 \pi+\cos 10 \pi=-1+1=0$ and $f(\pi)=\cos 9 \pi+\cos 10 \pi=0$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) & =\cos \frac{9 \pi}{2}+\cos \frac{10 \pi}{2} \\ & =\cos \frac{9 \pi}{2}+\cos 5 \pi=0+(-1)=-1\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Set of values of $x$ lying in $[0,2 \pi]$ satisfying the inequality $|\sin x|\gt2 \sin ^2 x$ contains
questions.solution:
$|\sin x|\gt2 \sin ^2 x$
$\Rightarrow|\sin x|(2|\sin x|-1) \lt 0 \Rightarrow 0 \lt |\sin x| \lt \frac{1}{2}$
$\Rightarrow x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{6}\right) \cup\left(\frac{5 \pi}{6}, \pi\right) \cup\left(\pi, \frac{7 \pi}{6}\right) \cup\left(\frac{11 \pi}{6}, 2 \pi\right)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The vertex connectivity of any tree is
questions.solution: The vertex connectivity of any tree is one.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The domain and range of $f(x)=\sin ^{-1}[x]$ are
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\sin ^{-1}[x]$ is defined, if $-1 \leq[x] \leq 1$
$\Rightarrow \quad[x]=\{-1,0,1\}$
$\therefore \quad x \in[-1,2)$
$\therefore$ Range is $\left\{\sin ^{-1}(-1), \sin ^{-1} 0, \sin ^{-1} 1\right\}$
$=\left\{-\frac{\pi}{2}, 0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right\}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $x$ is real, then the value of $\frac{x^2+34 x-71}{x^2+2 x-7}$ does not lie between
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } y=\frac{x^2+34 x-71}{x^2+2 x-7} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x^2(y-1)+2(y-17) x+(71-7 y)=0\end{aligned}$
For real values of $x$, its discriminant, $D \geq 0$
$\therefore \quad 4(y-17)^2-4(y-1)(71-7 y) \geq 0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \left(y^2-34 y+289\right) \\ & -\left(71 y-7 y^2-71+7 y\right) \geq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & y^2-14 y+45 \geq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & (y-5)(y-9) \geq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & y \leq 5 \text { and } y \geq 9\end{array}$
Hence, $y$ does not lie between 5 and 9

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $f(x)=|\cos x|$ and $g(x)=[x]$, then $g \circ f(x)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $f(x)=|\cos x|$ and $g(x)=[x]$,
$\therefore \quad g \circ f(x)=g(|\cos x|)$
$=[|\cos x|]$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: In the group $G=\left(\{1,2,3,4\}, x_5\right)$ the solution of $2^{-1} \times\left(3 \times{ }_5 x\right)=4$ is
questions.solution:
The composition table is as given below
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c|c}x_5 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\\hline 1 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \\2 & 2 & 4 & 1 & 3 \\3 & 3 & 1 & 4 & 2 \\4 & 4 & 3 & 2 & 1\end{array}
Clearly, identity element = 1
$\therefore \quad 2^{-1} \times_5\left(3 \times_5 x\right)=4$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & 3 \times_5\left(3 \times_5 x\right)=4 \\ \Rightarrow & \left(3 \times_5 3\right) \times_5 x=4\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{lr}\Rightarrow & 4 \times_5 x=4 \\ \Rightarrow & x=4^{-1} \times_5 4=1\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The range of the function $f(x)=\log _e\left(3 x^2-4 x+5\right)$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f(x) \text { is defined, if } 3 x^2-4 x+5\gt0 \\ & \Rightarrow  3\left[x^2-\frac{4}{3} x+\frac{5}{3}\right]\gt0\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow  3\left[\left(x-\frac{2}{3}\right)^2+\frac{11}{9}\right]\gt0$
which is true for all real $x$.
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \text { Domain of } f=(-\infty, \infty) \\
& \text { Let } y=\log _e\left(3 x^2-4 x+5\right) \Rightarrow e^y=3 x^2-4 x+5 \\
& \Rightarrow 3 x^2-4 x+\left(5-e^y\right)=0
\end{aligned}$
For $x$ to be real,
$16-12\left(5-e^y\right) \geq 0$
$\Rightarrow 12 e^y \geq 44$
$\Rightarrow e^y \geq \frac{11}{3}$
$\Rightarrow y \geq \log _e \frac{11}{3}$
Range of $f=\left[\log _e \frac{11}{3}, \infty\right)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $f(x)$ is a non-negative continuous function for all $x \geq 1$ such that $f^{\prime}(x) \leq p f(x)$, where $p\gt0$ and $f(1)=0$, then $[f(\sqrt{e})+f(\sqrt{\pi})]$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$f^{\prime}(x)-p f(x) \leq 0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d}{d x} e^{-p x} f(x) \leq 0$
Let $\quad g(x)=e^{-p x} f(x)$
$g^{\prime}(x) \leq 0, \forall x \geq 1$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & g(x) \leq g(1), \forall x \geq 1 \\ \Rightarrow & g(x) \leq 0 \\ \Rightarrow & f(x) \leq 0\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\text { But given, } & f(x) \geq 0 \\ \therefore & f(x)=0, \forall x \geq 1\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The length of the longest interval, in which $f(x)=3 \sin x-4 \sin ^3 x$ is increasing, is
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=3 \sin x-4 \sin ^3 x=\sin 3 x$
Since, $\sin x$ is increasing in the interval $\left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]$
$\therefore \quad-\frac{\pi}{2} \leq 3 x \leq \frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow-\frac{\pi}{6} \leq x \leq \frac{\pi}{6}$
Thus, the length of interval $=\left[\frac{\pi}{6}-\left(-\frac{\pi}{6}\right)\right]=\frac{\pi}{3}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The function $f(x)=\max \{(1-x),(1+x), 2\}$, $x \in(-\infty, \infty)$ is equivalent to
questions.solution:
Given that,
$f(x)=\max \{(1-x), 2,(1+x)\}$
For $x \leq-1$, we find that $1-x \geq 2$ and $1-x \geq 1+x$
$\therefore \max \{(1-x), 2,(1+x)\}=1-x$
For $-1 \lt x \lt 1$, we find that
$0 \lt 1-x \lt 2$ and $0 \lt 1+x \lt 2$
$\therefore \max \{(1-x), 2,(1+x)\}=2$
For $x \geq 1$, we find that
$1+x \geq 2,1+x\gt1-x$
$\therefore \max \{(1-x), 2,(1+x)\}=1+x$
Hence, $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}1-x, & x \leq-1 \\ 2, & -1 \lt x \lt 1 \\ 1+x, & x \geq 1\end{array}\right.$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The period of the function $f(x)=|\sin 4 x|+|\cos 4 x|$ is
questions.solution:
Since, $|\sin x|+|\cos x|$ is a periodic function with period $\pi / 2$.
$\therefore f(x)=|\sin 4 x|+|\cos 4 x|$ is a periodic function with period $\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2} i e, \frac{\pi}{8}$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The domain of the function $f(x)=\frac{\sqrt{9-x^2}}{\sin ^{-1}(3-x)}$ is
questions.solution:
$\sqrt{9-x^2}$ is defined for
$9-x^2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow(3-x)(3+x) \geq 0$
$\Rightarrow (x-3)(x+3) \leq 0...(i)$
$\Rightarrow -3 \leq x \leq 3$
$\sin ^{-1}(3-x)$ defined for
$-1 \leq 3-x \leq 1$
$\Rightarrow -4 \leq-x \leq-2$
$\Rightarrow  2 \leq x \leq 4...(ii)$
Also, $\sin ^{-1}(3-x) \neq 0$
$\Rightarrow 3-x \neq 0$ or $x \neq 3...(iii)$
From Eqs. (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
The domain of $f=([1-3,3] \cap[2,4])-\{3\}=[2,3)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $A B C D$ is a rectangular field. A vertical lamp post of height 12 m stands at the corner $A$. If the angle of elevation of its top from $B$ is $60^{\circ}$ and from $C$ is $45^{\circ}$, then the area of the field is
questions.solution:
Let $A E$ is a vertical lamp post
In $\triangle A E C$, $\tan 45^{\circ}=\frac{A E}{A C}$ $\Rightarrow \quad A C=A E=12 \mathrm{~m}$ and in $\triangle A B E$, $\tan 60^{\circ}=\frac{A E}{A B}$ $\Rightarrow \quad A B=\frac{A E}{\sqrt{3}}=4 \sqrt{3}$ Now, $B C=\sqrt{A C^2-A B^2}$ $\begin{aligned} & =\sqrt{144-48}=\sqrt{96} \\ & =4 \sqrt{6}\end{aligned}$ $\therefore$ Area of rectangle $A B C D$ $\begin{aligned} & =A B \times B C \\ & =4 \sqrt{3} \times 4 \sqrt{6} \\ & =48 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m}^2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: A man is standing on the horizontal plane. The angle of elevation of top of the pole is $\alpha$. If he walks a distance double the height of the pole, then the elevation of the pole is $2 \alpha$. The value of $\alpha$ is
questions.solution:
Let the height of the pole be $B C=h \mathrm{~m}$. In $\triangle A B C$, $\tan 2 \alpha=\frac{h}{x}$ ....(i)
and in $\triangle D B C$, $\tan \alpha=\frac{h}{2 h+x}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \tan \alpha=\frac{\frac{h}{x}}{2\left(\frac{h}{x}\right)+1}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \tan \alpha=\frac{\tan 2 \alpha}{2 \tan 2 \alpha+1}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \tan \alpha(2 \tan 2 \alpha+1)=\tan 2 \alpha$ $\Rightarrow \tan \alpha\left[2 \times \frac{2 \tan \alpha}{1-\tan ^2 \alpha}+1\right]=\frac{2 \tan \alpha}{1-\tan ^2 \alpha}$ $\Rightarrow \quad\left[4 \tan \alpha+1-\tan ^2 \alpha\right]=2$ $\Rightarrow \quad \tan ^2 \alpha-4 \tan \alpha+1=0$ $=\frac{4 \pm \sqrt{12}}{2}=\frac{4 \pm 2 \sqrt{3}}{2}$ Taking '-' sign, we get $\Rightarrow \quad \tan \alpha=2-\sqrt{3}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \tan \alpha=\tan 15^{\circ}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \alpha=15^{\circ} \quad$ or $\frac{\pi}{12}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The height of the chimney when it is found that on walking towards it 50 m in the horizontal line through its base, the angle of elevation of its top changes from $30^{\circ}$ to $60^{\circ}$, is
questions.solution:
Let $P Q=h$ be the height of chimney. $A$ and $B$ are the two points 50 m apart.
In $\triangle A P Q$, $\begin{aligned} \tan 30^{\circ} & =\frac{h}{A P} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & A P =h \cot 30^{\circ} \quad \text{....(i)} \end{aligned}$ and in $\triangle Q B P$, $\begin{array}{rlrl} &\tan 60^{\circ}=\frac{h}{B P} \\ \Rightarrow & B P =h \cot 60^{\circ} \quad \text{....(ii)} \\ \because & A P-B P =50 \\ \therefore & h\left(\cot 30^{\circ}-\cot 60^{\circ}\right)=50 \\ \Rightarrow h & =\frac{50}{\left(\sqrt{3}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\right)}=\frac{50 \sqrt{3}}{3-1} \\ & =\frac{50 \sqrt{3}}{2}=25 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m} \end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Each side of a square subtends an angle of $60^{\circ}$ at the top of a tower $h$ metre high standing in the centre of the square. If $a$ is the length of each side of the square, then
questions.solution:
Let $A B C D$ be a square of each side of length $a$. It is given that $\angle O C P=60^{\circ}$. Length of diagonal $A C=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a \sqrt{2}$ $\therefore \quad P C=\frac{A C}{2}=\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$

In $\quad \triangle O C P, \tan 60^{\circ}=\frac{O P}{P C}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \sqrt{3}=\frac{h}{a / \sqrt{2}} \\ \Rightarrow & \sqrt{3} a =\sqrt{2} h \\ \Rightarrow & 3 a^2 =2 h^2\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Which one of the following is independent of $\alpha$ in the hyperbola $\quad(0 \lt \alpha \lt \pi / 2)$ $\frac{x^2}{\cos ^2 \alpha}-\frac{y^2}{\sin ^2 \alpha}=1$
questions.solution:
$e^2=1+\frac{b^2}{a^2}=1+\frac{\sin ^2 \alpha}{\cos ^2 \alpha}=\frac{1}{\cos ^2 \alpha}$
and $\quad a^2=\cos ^2 \alpha$
$\therefore \quad a^2 e^2=1$
$\therefore$ foci $( \pm a e, 0)=( \pm 1,0)$, which is independent of $\alpha$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Consider the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ the area of the triangle formed by the asymptotes and the tangent drawn to it at $(a, 0)$ is
questions.solution:
Equation of tangent at $(a, 0)$ is $x=a$ and the point of intersection of $x=a$ and the asymptotes will be obtained by solving $x=a$ and the equation of asymptotes $b x \pm a y=0$
$\therefore a b \pm a y=0$
$\Rightarrow  y= \pm b$
Now, point of intersections are $(a, b)$ and $(a,-b)$.
$\therefore$ Area of triangle $=\frac{1}{2}(a b \times 2)=a b$ sq unit

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The eccentricity of the conic $9 x^2-16 y^2=144$ is
questions.solution:
Given conic is $9 x^2-16 y^2=144$
$\begin{array}{lc}\Rightarrow & \frac{x^2}{16}-\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \\ \therefore & e=\sqrt{1+\frac{b^2}{a^2}}=\sqrt{1+\frac{9}{16}}=\frac{5}{4}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: All the chords of the hyperbola $3 x^2-y^2-2 x+4 y=0$, subtending a right angle at the origin pass through the fixed point
questions.solution:
Let $a x+b y=1$ be the chord  ....(i)
Making the equation of hyperbola homogeneous using Eq. (i), we get
$3 x^2-y^2+(-2 x+4 y)(a x+b y)=0$
or $\quad(3-2 a) x^2+(-1+4 b) y^2+(-2 b+4 a) x y=0$
Since, the angle subtended at the origin is a right angle.
$\therefore$ Coefficient of $x^2+$ Coefficient of $y^2=0$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & (3-2 a)+(-1+4 b) \\ \Rightarrow & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & a=2 b+1\end{array}$
$\therefore$ Chords are $(2 b+1) x+b y-1=0$
or $\quad b(2+y)+(x-1)=0$,
which clearly pass through the fixed point $(1,-2)$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The distance between the foci of a hyperbola is double the distance between its vertices and the length of its conjugate axis is 6 .The equation of the hyperbola referred to its axes as axes of coordinates are
questions.solution:
According to given condition, $2 a e=2 \cdot 2 a$
$\Rightarrow \quad e=2$
and $\quad 2 b=6 \Rightarrow b=3$
Hence, $a=\frac{3}{\sqrt{3}}=\sqrt{3}$
$\therefore$ Required equation is
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{x^2}{3}-\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 3 x^2-y^2=9\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If the equation $l x^2+2 m x y+n y^2=0$ represents a pair conjugate diameter of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$, then
questions.solution:
Let $y=m_1 x$ and $y=m_2 x$ be the lines represented by $l x^2+2 m x y+n y^2=0$, then
$m_1 m_2=\frac{l}{n}...(i)$
But $y=m_1 x$ and $y=m_2 x$ are conjugate diameters of the hyperbola.
$\therefore  m_1 m_2=\frac{b^2}{a^2}...(ii)$
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$\frac{l}{n}=\frac{b^2}{a^2} \Rightarrow l a^2=n b^2$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $e$ is the eccentricity of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ and $\theta$ is the angle between the asymptotes, then $\cos \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The equations of the asymptotes of the hyperbola
$\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ are $y= \pm \frac{b}{a} i e$,
$y=\frac{b}{a} x$ and $y=\frac{-b}{a} x$
If $\theta$ is the angle between the two asymptotes,
then, $\tan \theta=\frac{\frac{b}{a}+\frac{b}{a}}{1-\frac{b}{a} \cdot \frac{b}{a}}=\frac{2 b}{a} \times \frac{a^2}{a^2-b^2}=\frac{2 a b}{a^2-b^2}$
From this, we get $\cos \theta=\frac{a^2-b^2}{a^2+b^2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 \cos ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}-1=\frac{a^2-b^2}{a^2+b^2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 \cos ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=1+\frac{a^2-b^2}{a^2+b^2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 \cos ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{2 a^2}{a^2+b^2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \cos ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{a^2}{a^2+b^2}$
But $b^2=a^2\left(e^2-1\right)=a^2 e^2-a^2 \Rightarrow a^2+b^2=a^2 e^2$
$\therefore \quad \cos ^2\left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{a^2}{a^2 e^2}=\frac{1}{e^2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \cos \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)=\frac{1}{e}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The tangent and normal to a rectangular hyperbola $x y=c^2$ at a point cuts off intercepts $a_1$ and $a_2$ on one axis and $b_1, b_2$ on the other, then $a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let the point be $P\left(c t, \frac{c}{t}\right)$
The equation of the tangent to the hyperbola $x y=c^2$ at $P$ is $x \frac{c}{t}+c t y=2 c^2$
$\Rightarrow  x+y t^2-2 c t=0...(i)$
Then, putting $y=0, x=0 \backslash$ successively in Eq. (i), we get $a_1=2 c t$ and $b_1=\frac{2 c}{t}$
$\left(\because\right.$ intercepts of tangent on the axes are $a_1$ and $b_1$ )
Again the equation of the normal to the hyperbola $x y=c^2$ at $P$ is
$x t^3-y t-c t^4+c=0$
As before, $a_2=c t-\frac{c}{t^3}$ and $b_2=-c t^3+\frac{c}{t}$
$\therefore a_1 a_2+b_1 b_2=2 c t\left(c t-\frac{c}{t^3}\right)+\frac{2 c}{t}\left(-c t^3+\frac{c}{t}\right)$
$=0$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The diameter of $16 x^2-9 y^2=144$ which is conjugate to $x=2 y$ is
questions.solution:
The diameters $y=m_1 x$ and $y=m_2 x$ are conjugate diameters of the hyperbola
$\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$, if $m_1 m_2=\frac{b^2}{a^2}$
Given equation is
$\frac{x^2}{9}-\frac{y^2}{16}=1$
Here, $a^2=9, b^2=16$
Also given, $x=2 y$
Then, $\quad m_1=\frac{1}{2}$
$\because \quad \frac{1}{2} \times m_2=\frac{16}{9}$
$\Rightarrow \quad m_2=\frac{32}{9}$
Hence, the required equation of diameter is
$y=\frac{32 x}{9}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If the chords of the hyperbola $x^2-y^2=a^2$ touch the parabola $y^2=4 a x$. Then, the locus of the middle points of these chords is

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $P Q$ is a double ordinate of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ such that $O P Q$ is an equilateral triangle, $O$ bing the centre of the hyperbola, then the eccentricity $e$ of the hyperbola satisfies.
questions.solution:
Let the hyperbola be $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ and any double ordinate $P Q$ be $\left(a \sec \theta_1 b \tan \theta\right),\left(a \sec \theta_1-b \tan \theta\right)$ and $O$ is centre $(0,0)$.
$\triangle O P^{\prime} Q$ being equilateral. $\therefore \quad \tan 30^{\circ}=\frac{b \tan \theta}{a \sec \theta}$ $\begin{array}{lr}\Rightarrow & 3 \cdot \frac{b^2}{a^2}=\operatorname{cosce}^2 \theta \\ \Rightarrow & 3\left(e^2-1\right)=\operatorname{cosce}^2 \theta\end{array}$ Now, $\quad \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta \geq 1$ $\therefore \quad 3\left(e^2-1\right) \geq 1 \Rightarrow e^2 \geq \frac{4}{3}$ $\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & e\gt2 / \sqrt{3}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The difference of the focal distance of any point on the hyperbola is equal to its
options: half the length of the transverse axis
questions.solution: The difference of the focal distance at any point on the hyperbola is same as length of transverse axis.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\int \frac{\cos x+x \cos x}{x^2+x \cos x} d x$ is
questions.solution:
$\int \frac{\cos x+x \sin x}{x^2+x \cos x} d x$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int \frac{x+\cos x-x+x \sin x}{x^2+x \cos x} d x \\ & =\int \frac{d x}{x}-\int \frac{x(1-\sin x)}{x(x+\cos x)} d x\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\log x-\log (x+\cos x)+C \\ & =\log \left|\frac{x}{x+\cos x}\right|+C\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $I_n=\int \sin ^n x d x$, then $n I_n-(n-1) I_{n-2}$ equals
questions.solution:
We know that, if $I_n=\int \sin ^n x d x$, then
$I_n=\frac{\sin ^{n-1} x \cos x}{n}+\frac{n-1}{n} I_{n-2}$
where $n$ is a positive integer.
$\Rightarrow n I_n-(n-1) I_{n-2}=-\sin ^{n-1} x \cos x$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int f(x) d x=g(x)$, then $\int \cos x f(\sin x) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Here, $\int f(x) d x=g(x)$
Now, $\int \cos x f(\sin x) d x$
Let, $\sin x=k \Rightarrow \cos x d x=d k$
$\therefore \int f(k) d k=g(k)+C=g(\sin x)+C$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\sin x \cos x} d x=\int \frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{\tan x} \sec ^2 x d x \\ = & \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\tan x}} \cdot \sec ^2 x d x=\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}}{1 / 2}=2 \sqrt{\tan x}+c\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
If $\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right\} d x$
$=A \sin ^{-1} x+B x \sqrt{1-x^2}+C$
then $A+B$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right\} d x=A \sin ^{-1} x+B x \sqrt{1-x^2}+C$
Let $\quad I=\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right\} d x$
Put $x=\cos 2 \theta \Rightarrow d x=-2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$\therefore I=-\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-\cos 2 \theta}{1+\cos 2 \theta}}\right\} 2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$=-\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2 \sin ^2 \theta}{2 \cos ^2 \theta}}\right\} 2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$=-\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\tan ^2 \theta}\right\} 2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$=-\int \sin \left\{2 \tan ^{-1} \tan \theta\right\} 2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$=-\int[\sin (2 \theta)] 2 \sin 2 \theta d \theta$
$=-\int 2 \sin ^2 2 \theta d \theta=-\int(1-\cos 4 \theta) d \theta$
$=-\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 4 \theta}{4}\right]+C$
$=\left[-\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{4} \times 2 \sin 2 \theta \cos 2 \theta\right]+C$
$=\left[-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} x\right)+\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{1-x^2} \times x\right]+C$
$=\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{1-x^2}+\left(C-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$
But $I=A \sin ^{-1} x+B x \sqrt{1-x^2}+C$
$\therefore \quad A=\frac{1}{2}$ and $B=\frac{1}{2}$
Hence, $\quad A+B=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}=1$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int\left(e^{a \log x}+e^{x \log a}\right) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \int\left(e^{a \log x}+e^{x \log a}\right) d x=\int\left(e^{\log x^a}+e^{\log a^x}\right) d x \\ = & \int\left(x^a+a^x\right) d x=\frac{x^{a+1}}{a+1}+\frac{a^x}{\log _e a}+c\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{1-x^2}{\left(1+x^2\right) \sqrt{1+x^4}} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $\begin{aligned} I & =\int \frac{1-x^2}{\left(1+x^2\right) \sqrt{1+x^4}} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\frac{1}{x^2}-1}{\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right) \sqrt{x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}}} d x\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =-\int \frac{1}{\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right) \sqrt{\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)^2-(\sqrt{2})^2}} d\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right) \\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \operatorname{cosec}^{-1}\left(\frac{x+\frac{1}{x}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+C \\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2} x}{x^2+1}\right)+C\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{(\sec x+\tan x)^{9 / 2}} d x$ equals
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{(\sec x+\tan x)^{9 / 2}} d x$
Put $\sec x+\tan x=t$
Then, $\quad \sec x-\tan x=\frac{1}{t}$
and $\quad \sec x(\sec x+\tan x) d x=d t$
$\Rightarrow \quad \sec x d x=\frac{1}{t} d t$
Also, $\quad \sec x=\frac{1}{2}\left(t+\frac{1}{t}\right)$
$\therefore \quad I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\frac{1}{t}\left(t+\frac{1}{t}\right)}{t^{9 / 2}} d t$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{t^{9 / 2}}+\frac{1}{t^{13 / 2}} d t \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=-\frac{1}{7 t^{7 / 2}}-\frac{1}{11 t^{11 / 2}}+K \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=-\frac{1}{t^{11 / 2}\left\{\frac{t^2}{7}+\frac{1}{11}\right\}+K} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=-\frac{1}{(\sec x+\tan x)^{11 / 2}} \\ & \left\{\frac{1}{11}+\frac{1}{7}(\sec x+\tan x)^2\right\}+K\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\int \frac{1}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cos x} d x$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int \frac{1}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cos x} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{\cos \frac{\pi}{3}} \int \frac{\cos \left\{x-\left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right\}}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cos x} d x \\ & =2 \int \frac{\cos x \cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\sin x \sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)}{\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \cos x} d x \\ & =2 \int\left\{\cot \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan x\right\} d x \\ & =2\left\{\log \left|\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)\right|+\log |\sec x|\right\}+C \\ & =2 \log \left|\sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) \sec x\right|+C\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\cdot \int \frac{\cos x}{\sin ^2 x \cdot(\sin x+\cos x)} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let, $I=\int \frac{\cos x}{\sin ^2 x(\sin x+\cos x)} d x$ On dividing numerator and denominator by $\cos ^3 x$, we get $I=\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{\tan ^2 x(1+\tan x)} d x$
Put $\quad \tan x=t \Rightarrow \sec ^2 x d x=d t$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I & =\int \frac{d t}{t^2(1+t)} \\ & =\int\left[-\frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{t^2}+\frac{1}{1+t}\right] d t \end{aligned}$ (using partial fraction) $\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad I & =-\int \frac{1}{t} d t+\int t^{-2} d t+\int \frac{1}{1+t} d t \\ & =-\log |t|-\frac{1}{t}+\log |1+t|+C \\ & =-\frac{1}{\tan x}+\log \left|\frac{1+\tan x}{\tan x}\right|+C \\ & =-\cot x+\log \left|\frac{1+\tan x}{\tan x}\right|+C \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{x^2-1}{\left(x^4+3 x^2+1\right) \tan ^{-1}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let, $I=\int \frac{x^2-1}{\left(x^4+3 x^2+1\right) \tan ^{-1}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)} d x$ On dividing numerator and denominator by $x^2$, we get $I=\int \frac{1-\frac{1}{x^2}}{\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}+3\right) \tan ^{-1}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)} d x$
Put $\quad x+\frac{1}{x}=t$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left(1-\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x \\ & =d t \text { and } \\ & \begin{aligned} x^2+\frac{1}{x^2} & =t^2-2 \\ \quad I & =\int \frac{d t}{\left(t^2-2+3\right) \tan ^{-1} t} \\ & =\int \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^2\right) \tan ^{-1} t} \\ & =\log \left(\tan ^{-1} t\right)+C \\ & =\log \left[\tan ^{-1}\left(x+\frac{1}{x}\right)\right]+C \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{\sin x-\cos x+\sqrt{2}}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let, } I=\int \frac{d x}{\sin x-\cos x+\sqrt{2}} \\ & \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow I=\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin x-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos x\right)+\sqrt{2}} \\ &= \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \frac{d x}{1-\cos \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int \frac{d x}{2 \sin ^2\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{\pi}{8}\right)} \\ &=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \int \frac{d x}{\sin ^2\left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{\pi}{8}\right)}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \\ &= \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\left[\frac{-\cot \left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{\pi}{8}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+C \\ &=-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cot \left(\frac{x}{2}+\frac{\pi}{8}\right)+C\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int x^x \log (e x) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int x^x($ logex $) d x$
$=\int x^x(1+\log x) d x$
let $t=x^x=e^{x \log x}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d t}{d x}=x^x\left\{x \cdot \frac{1}{x}+\log x\right\}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & d t=x^x(1+\log x) d x \\ \therefore & I=\int t+c=x^x+c .\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\int \frac{\text { Th }}{x^2\left(x^n+1\right)^{\frac{1-1)}{n}}}:=-[f(x)]^{1 / n}+c$ then $f(x)$ is
questions.solution:
We have;
$\int \frac{d x}{x^2\left(x^n+1\right)^{\frac{(n-1)}{n}}}=\int \frac{d x}{x^2 \cdot x^{n-1}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^n}\right)^{\frac{(n-1)}{n}}}$
$=\int \frac{d x}{x^{n+1}\left(1+x^{-n}\right)^{\frac{(n-1)}{n}}}$
Put, $1+x^{-n}=t$
$\therefore \quad-n x^{-n-1} d x=d t$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d x}{x^{n+1}}=\frac{-d t}{n}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \int \frac{d x}{x^2\left(x^n+1\right)^{\frac{(n-1)}{n}}}=-\frac{1}{n} \int \frac{d t}{t^{\frac{(n-1)}{n}}}$
$=-\frac{1}{n} \int t^{(1 / n)-1} d t=-\frac{1}{n} \cdot \frac{t^{\left(\frac{1}{n}-1+1\right)}}{\left(\frac{1}{n}-1+1\right)}+c$
$=-t^{1 / n}+c=-\left(1+x^{-n}\right)^{\frac{1}{n}}+c$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \sqrt{1+\operatorname{cosec} x} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \sqrt{1+\operatorname{cosec} x} d x=\int \frac{\sqrt{1+\sin x}}{\sqrt{\sin x}} d x$
$=\int \pm \frac{\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}}{\sqrt{2 \sin \frac{x}{2} \cdot \cos \frac{x}{2}}} \cdot d x$
$= \pm \int \frac{\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}}{\sqrt{1-\left(\sin \frac{x}{2}-\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}} \cdot d x$
Put, $\quad \sin \frac{x}{2}-\cos \frac{x}{2}=t$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}+\sin \frac{x}{2}\right) d x=2 d t$
$\therefore \quad I= \pm \int \frac{2 d t}{\sqrt{1-t^2}}= \pm 2 \sin ^{-1} t+c$
$= \pm 2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{x}{2}-\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)+c$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{d x}{x^2+4 x+13}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int \frac{d x}{x^2+4 x+13} \\ & =\int \frac{d x}{x^2+4 x+4+3^2}=\int \frac{d x}{\left(x+2)^2+3^2\right.} \\ & =\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+2}{3}\right)+c\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\int f(x) d x=F(x)$, then $\int x^3 f\left(x^2\right) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  We have, } \int f(x) d x=F(x) \\ & \therefore \int x^3 f\left(x^2\right) d x=\frac{1}{2} \int x^2 f\left(x^2\right) d\left(x^2\right)\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Evaluate $\int \frac{(x-1) e^x}{(x+1)^3} d x$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
\text { Let } \quad & \quad I=\int \frac{(x-1) e^x}{(x+1)^3} d x \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \quad I=\int\left\{\frac{x+1-2}{(x+1)^3}\right\} e^x d x \\
& =\int\left\{\frac{1}{(x+1)^2}-\frac{2}{(x+1)^3}\right\} e^x d x \\
& \pm \int e^x \cdot \frac{1}{(x+1)^2} d x-2 \int e^x \frac{1}{(x+1)^3} d x
\end{aligned}$
Applying integrating by parts, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& \begin{array}{l}
=\left(\frac{1}{(x+1)^2} e^x-\int e^x \frac{(-2)}{(x+1)^3} d x\right) -2 \int e^x \frac{1}{(x+1)^3} d x \\
=\frac{e^x}{(x+1)^2}+C
\end{array}
\end{aligned}$
Note We can use the formula
$\int e^x\left[f(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right] d x=e^x f(x)+C$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
If $\frac{1}{x(x+1)(x+2) \ldots(x+n)}=\frac{A_0}{x}+\frac{A_1}{x+1}$
$+\frac{A_2}{x+2}+\ldots+\frac{A_n}{x+n}$ then $A_r$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{x(x+1)(x+2) \ldots(x+n)}= \frac{A_0}{x}+\frac{A_1}{x+1}+\ldots+ \\ & \frac{A_t}{x+r}+\ldots+\frac{A_n}{x+n}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 1=A_0(x+1)(x+2) \ldots(x+n) \\ & +\ldots+A_r x(x+1)(x+2) \ldots(x+r-1)(x+r+1) \\ & \quad \ldots(x+n)+\ldots+A_n x(x+1) \ldots(x+n-1)\end{aligned}$
Putting $x=-r$, we get;
$\begin{array}{r}1=A_r(-r)(-r+1)(-r+2) \ldots(-3)(-2)(-1)(1)(2) \\ \ldots(n-r)\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow 1=A_r(-1)^r\{r(r-1)(r-2) \ldots(3 \cdot 2 \cdot 1\} \\ (1 \cdot 2 \cdot 3 \ldots(n-r))\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad A_r=(-1)^r / r!(n-r)!$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Evaluate $\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x$
questions.solution:
$\text { Let } \begin{aligned}
I & =\int(\sqrt{\tan x}+\sqrt{\cot x}) d x \\
& =\int \frac{\tan x+1}{\sqrt{\tan x}} d x
\end{aligned}$
Put $\tan x=t^2$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \sec ^2 x d x=2 t d t \\ & \Rightarrow \quad d x=\frac{2 t d t}{1+\tan ^2 t}=\frac{2 t}{1+t^4} d t \\ & \begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I & =\int \frac{t^2+1}{\sqrt{t^2}} \cdot \frac{2 t}{t^4+1} d t \\ & =2 \int \frac{t^2+1}{t^4+1} d t\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} &=2 \int \frac{1+\frac{1}{t^2}}{t^2+\frac{1}{t^2}-2+2} d t \\ &=2 \int \frac{1+\frac{1}{t^2}}{\left(t-\frac{1}{t}\right)^2+(\sqrt{2})^2} d t \\ &=2 \int \frac{d u}{u^2+(\sqrt{2})^2} \\ & \text { where, } \quad \begin{aligned} u & =t-\frac{1}{t} \Rightarrow d u=\left(1+\frac{1}{t^2}\right) d t \\ \Rightarrow \quad I & =\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{u}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+C \\ & =\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\tan x}-\sqrt{\cot x}}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+C\end{aligned} \$\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \cos ^{-3 / 7} x \sin ^{-11 / 7} x d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$m+n=-\frac{3}{7}+\left(\frac{-11}{7}\right)=-2(-$ ve integer $)$
Let $I=\int \cos ^{-3 / 7} x\left(\sin ^{\left(-2+\frac{3}{7}\right)} x\right) d x$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int \cos ^{-3 / 7} x \sin ^{-2} x \sin ^{3 / 7} x d x \\ & =\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}{\left(\frac{\cos ^{3 / 7} x}{\sin ^{3 / 7} x}\right)} d x=\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x d x}{\cot ^{3 / 7} x}\end{aligned}$
Put $\cos x=t \Rightarrow-\operatorname{cosec}^2 x d x=d t$
$\therefore \quad I=-\int \frac{d t}{t^{3 / 7}}=-\frac{t^{\frac{3}{7}+1}}{-\frac{3}{7}+1}+C$
$\begin{aligned} & =-\frac{7}{4} t^{4 / 7}+c=-\frac{7}{4} \cot ^{4 / 7} x+C \\ & =-\frac{7}{4} \tan ^{-4 / 7} x+C\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{3+\sin 2 x} d x$ is
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{3+\sin 2 x} d x$
$=\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{4+\sin 2 x-1} d x$
$\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{4-(\sin x-\cos x)^2} d x$
Put $\sin x-\cos x=t$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad(\cos x+\sin x) d x=d t \\ & \therefore \quad I=\int \frac{d t}{4-t^2}=\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{2-t}{2+t}+C\end{aligned}$
$=\frac{1}{4} \log \left(\frac{2-\sin x+\cos x}{2+\sin x-\cos x}\right)+C$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{2 d x}{\left(e^x+e^{-x}\right)^2}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\text { Let } \begin{aligned}
I & =\int \frac{2 d x}{\left(e^x+e^{-x}\right)^2}=\int \frac{2 d x}{e^{2 x}+e^{-2 x}+2} \\
& =\int \frac{2 e^{2 x} d x}{e^{4 x}+2 e^{2 x}+1}
\end{aligned}$
Put $\quad e^{2 x}=t$
$\Rightarrow \quad 2 e^{2 x} d x=d t$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I & =\int \frac{d t}{t^2+2 t+1}=\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)^2}=-\frac{1}{t+1}+c \\ & =-\frac{1}{e^{2 x}+1}=-\frac{e^{-x}}{e^x+e^{-x}}+c\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\int f(x) d x=g(x)$, then $\int x^9 \cdot f\left(x^5\right) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $\int f(x) d x=g(x)$
Let $\quad I=\int x^9 \int\left(x^5\right) d x$ Put $x^5=t \Rightarrow 5 x^4 d x=d t$ $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{5} \int t f(t) d t \\ & =\frac{1}{5}\left[t \int f(t) d t-\int[f(t) d t]\right. \end{aligned}$ [integrating by part] $\begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{5}\left[t g(t)-\int g(t) d t\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{5}\left[x^2 g\left(x^5\right)-5 \int g\left(x^5\right) x^4 d x\right]+C \\ & =\frac{x^5}{5} g\left(x^5\right)-\int x^4 g\left(x^5\right) d x+C \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\int \frac{d x}{x \sqrt{1-(\log x)^2}}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int \frac{d x}{x \sqrt{1-(\log x)^2}} \\ & \text { Put } \log x=t \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{x} d x=d t \\ & \begin{aligned} & \therefore I=\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1-t^2}}=\sin ^{-1} t+C \\ &=\sin ^{-1}(\log x)+C\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{x^2}{\left(x^2+2\right)\left(x^2+3\right)} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\int \frac{x^2}{\left(x^2+2\right)\left(x^2+3\right)} d x=\int\left(\frac{3}{x^2+3}-\frac{2}{x^2+2}\right) d x$
$=\frac{3}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-\frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+C$
$=\sqrt{3} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}\right)-\sqrt{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+C$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\int \frac{x^2+4}{x^4+16} d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\int \frac{x^2+4}{x^4+16} d x$
$=\int \frac{1+\frac{4}{x^2}}{x^2+\frac{16}{x^2}} d x=\int \frac{d\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)}{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)^2+8}$
$=\int \frac{d t}{t^2+(2 \sqrt{2})^2}$, where $t=x-\frac{4}{x}$
$=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2-4}{2 \sqrt{2} x}\right)+c$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\int \frac{2^x}{\sqrt{1-4^x}} d x=k \sin ^{-1}\left(2^x\right)+C$, then $k$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $\quad I=\int \frac{2^x}{\sqrt{1-4^x}} d x$
Let $\quad 2^x=t$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad 2^x \log 2 d x=d t \\
& \therefore \quad I=\frac{1}{\log 2} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-t^2}} d t \\
& =\frac{1}{\log 2} \sin ^{-1}(t)+C \\
& =\frac{1}{\log 2} \sin ^{-1}\left(2^x\right)+C
\end{aligned}$
But $\quad I=k \sin ^{-1}\left(2^x\right)+C$,
$\Rightarrow \quad k=\frac{1}{\log 2}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\int f(x) d x=F(x)$, then $\int x^3 f\left(x^2\right) d x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have, $\int f(x) d x=F(x)$
$\therefore \int x^3 f\left(x^2\right) d x=\frac{1}{2} \int x^2 f\left(x^2\right) d\left(x^2\right)$
$=\frac{1}{2}\left[x^2 F\left(x^2\right)-\int F\left(x^2\right) d\left(x^2\right)\right]$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\frac{2 x+3}{(x+1)(x-3)}=\frac{a}{x+1}+\frac{b}{x-3}$, then $a+b$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that,
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{2 x+3}{(x+1)(x-3)}=\frac{a}{(x+1)}+\frac{b}{(x-3)} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & (2 x+3)=a(x-3)+b(x+1)\end{aligned}$
On comparing the coefficient of $x$ and constant, we get
$\begin{aligned} & 2=a+b \text { and } 3=-3 a+b \\ \Rightarrow \quad & a=-\frac{1}{4} \text { and } b=\frac{9}{4}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad a+b=-\frac{1}{4}+\frac{9}{4}=2$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\frac{e^x+2}{\left(e^x-1\right)\left(2 e^x-3\right)}=-\frac{3}{e^x-1}+\frac{B}{2 e^x-3}$, then $B$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Here, $e^x+2=-3\left(2 e^x-3\right)+B\left(e^x-1\right)$
On equating the coefficient of $e^x$ and constant, we get
$\begin{aligned} & 1=-6+B \\ & 2=9-B \Rightarrow B=7\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\frac{2 x+3}{(x+1)(x-3)}=\frac{a}{x+1}+\frac{b}{x-3}$, then $a+b$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\therefore(2 x+3)=a(x-3)+b(x+1)$
Put $x=-1$ and $x=3$ respectively, we get
$1=-4 a$ and $9=4 b$
$\Rightarrow \quad a=-\frac{1}{4}$ and $b=\frac{9}{4}$
$\therefore \quad a+b=-\frac{1}{4}+\frac{9}{4}=2$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Evaluate $\int \frac{1}{(x+1) \sqrt{x^2-1}} d x$
questions.solution:
Let $I=\int \frac{1}{(x+1) \sqrt{x^2-1}} d x$
Put, $x+1=\frac{1}{t}$
$\Rightarrow  d x=-\frac{1}{t^2} d t$, then
$I=\int \frac{1}{\frac{1}{t} \sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{t}-1\right)^2-1}}\left(\frac{-1}{t^2}\right) d t$
$\Rightarrow  I=-\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1-2 t}}=-\int(1-2 t)^{-1 / 2} d t$
$=-\frac{(1-2 t)^{1 / 2}}{(-2)\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}+c$
$\Rightarrow  I=\sqrt{1-2 t}+c$
$\Rightarrow I=\sqrt{1-\frac{2}{x+1}}+c$
$\Rightarrow  I=\sqrt{\frac{x-1}{x+1}}+c$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\sin ^{-1} x+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$, then value of $x$ will be
questions.solution:
Given, $\sin ^{-1} x+\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \sin ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \sin ^{-1} x=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) \\ & {\left[\because \tan ^{-1} \theta+\cot ^{-1} \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}\right] }\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & x=\sin \left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right\} \\ \Rightarrow & x=\sin \left\{\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right)\right\} \\ \Rightarrow & x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\cos ^{-1}\left\{\cos 2 \cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{2}-1)\right\}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  } \cos ^{-1}\left[\cos \left\{2 \cdot \cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{2}-1\}\right]\right. \\ & \left.=\cos ^{-1}[\cos \{2 \times 67.5\}]\right] \\ & =\cos ^{-1}\left[\cos 135^{\circ}\right]=135^{\circ}=\frac{3 \pi}{4}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $3 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)-4 \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)$ $+2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{3}$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given,
$\begin{aligned}
& 3 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)-4 \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right) \\
& +2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1-x^2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{3} \\
\Rightarrow & 6 \tan ^{-1} x-8 \tan ^{-1} x+4 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{3} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & 2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{3} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{6} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & x=\tan \frac{\pi}{6}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi$, then the value of $x+y+z-x y z$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+y+z-x y z}{1-x y-y z-z x}\right) \\ \Rightarrow & x+y+z-x y z=0\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\tan \left[2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{5}\right)-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]$ is
questions.solution:
$\tan \left[2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{5}\right)-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]=\tan \left[\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{2}{5}}{1-\frac{1}{25}}\right)-\frac{\pi}{4}\right]$
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\because 2 \tan ^{-1} \theta=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \theta}{1-\theta^2}\right)\right]} \\ & =\tan \left[\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{10}{24}\right)-\frac{\pi}{4}\right] \\ & =\frac{\tan \left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{10}{24}\right)\right\}-\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{1+\tan \left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{10}{24}\right)\right\} \cdot \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{\frac{10}{24}-1}{1+\frac{10}{24} \cdot 1} \\ & =\frac{10-24}{24+10} \\ & =\frac{-14}{34}=\frac{-7}{17}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\sec ^{-1} x=\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} y$, then $\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{x}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{y}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $\sec ^{-1} x=\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} y$
or $\quad \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)$
On adding $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)$ on both sides, we get $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{y}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$
$\left[\because \sin ^{-1} \theta+\cos ^{-1} \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$
Now, $\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1 / \sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{1-\frac{1}{5}}}\right)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}$ and $\cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1-\frac{9}{10}}}{\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}}\right)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3}$
$\therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}}{1-\frac{1}{6}}\right)=\tan ^{-1}(1)=\frac{\pi}{4}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Now, } \sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1 / \sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{1-\frac{1}{5}}}\right)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2} \\ & \text { and } \cos ^{-1} \frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1-\frac{9}{10}}}{\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}}\right)=\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3} \\ & \therefore \tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{1}{3}=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}}{1-\frac{1}{6}}\right)=\tan ^{-1}(1)=\frac{\pi}{4}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $x=\sin \left(2 \tan ^{-1} 2\right), y=\sin \left(\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{4}{3}\right)$, then
questions.solution:
we have \(x=\sin \left(2 \tan ^{-1} 2\right)\),
\(\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow x=\sin (2 \theta), \tan ^{-1} 2=\theta \\
& \Rightarrow x=\frac{2 \tan \theta}{1+\tan ^2 \theta}, \tan \theta=2 \\
& \Rightarrow x=\frac{4}{5}
\end{aligned}\)
Also, \(y=\sin \left(\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)\right)\)
\(\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & y=\sin (\theta), \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)=\theta \\
\Rightarrow & y=\sin (\theta), \tan (2 \theta)=\frac{4}{3} \\
\Rightarrow & y=\sin (\theta), \tan (\theta)=\frac{1}{2} \\
\Rightarrow & y=\frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}
\end{array}\)
Hence, \(y^2=\frac{1}{5}=1-\frac{4}{5}=1-x\)

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi$, then the value of $x+y+z-x y z$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  Given, }  \tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow  \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+y+z-x y z}{1-x y-y z-z x}\right)=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow  x+y+z-x y z=0\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} y=2 \pi$, then the value of $\sin ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} y$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $\cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} y=2 \pi$
$\Rightarrow  \frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} x+\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} y=2 \pi$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \pi-2 \pi=\sin ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} y \\ \Rightarrow & \sin ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} y=-\pi\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\theta=\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} x, \quad 1 \leq x \lt \infty$, then the smallest interval in which $\theta$ lies is
questions.solution:
$\theta=\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-\tan ^{-1} x$
$\left[\because \sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}\right]$
$\Rightarrow \quad \theta=\cot ^{-1} x$
Since, $1 \leq x \lt \infty$, therefore $0 \leq \theta \leq \frac{\pi}{4}$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $2 \tan ^{-1}(\cos x)=\tan ^{-1}(2 \operatorname{cosec} x)$, then the value of $x$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Given, } 2 \tan ^{-1}(\cos x)=\tan ^{-1}(2 \operatorname{cosec} x) \\ & \therefore \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \cos x}{1-\cos ^2 x}\right)=\tan ^{-1}(2 \operatorname{cosec} x)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 \cos x}{1-\cos ^2 x}=2 \operatorname{cosec} x \\ & \Rightarrow \sin x=\cos x \Rightarrow x=\frac{\pi}{4}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{x}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{b}{x}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Given, } \tan ^{-1} \frac{a}{x}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{b}{x}=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\left(\frac{a}{x}+\frac{b}{x}\right)}{1-\frac{a b}{x^2}}\right]=\frac{\pi}{2} \\ &  \end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{rlr}\Rightarrow & \frac{\frac{a+b}{x}}{x^2-a b} & =\tan \frac{\pi}{2} \\ \Rightarrow & x^2 & =a b \\ \Rightarrow & x & =\sqrt{a b}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{3}=\theta$, then $9 x^2-12 x y \cos \theta+4 y^2$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\cos ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{y}{3}=\theta$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \cos ^{-1}\left\{\frac{x y}{6}-\sqrt{1-\frac{x^2}{4}} \cdot \sqrt{1-\frac{y^2}{9}}\right\}=\theta \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x y-\sqrt{4-x^2} \cdot \sqrt{9-y^2}=6 \cos \theta \\ & \Rightarrow \quad(x y-6 \cos \theta)^2=\left(4-x^2\right)\left(9-y^2\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad-12 x y \cos \theta+36 \cos ^2 \theta=36-4 y^2-9 x^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 9 x^2+4 y^2-12 x y \cos \theta=36 \sin ^2 \theta\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\cos ^{-1} p+\cos ^{-1} q+\cos ^{-1} r=3 \pi$, then $p^2+q^2+r^2+2 p q r$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\cos ^{-1} p+\cos ^{-1} q+\cos ^{-1} r=3 \pi$
We know that, if $y=\cos ^{-1} x$, then $-1 \leq x \leq 1$ and $0 \leq y \leq \pi$.
Hence, the given equation will hold only when each is $\pi$.
$\therefore \quad p=q=r=\cos \pi=-1$
$\therefore \quad p^2+q^2+r^2+2 p q r$
$\begin{aligned} & =(-1)^2+(-1)^2+(-1)^2+2(-1)(-1)(-1) \\ & =1+1+1-2=3-2=1\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\sin ^{-1}\left\{\cot \left(\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}}\right.\right.$ $\left.\left.+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{12}}{4}+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right\}$ is
questions.solution:
We have, $\sin ^{-1}\left\{\cot \left(\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}}\right.\right.$
$\left.\left.+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{12}}{4}+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right\}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\sin ^{-1}\left\{\cot \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right\} \\ & =\sin ^{-1}\left\{\cot \left(\frac{\pi}{12}+\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right\} \\ & =\sin ^{-1}\left(\cot \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & =\sin ^{-1} 0=0\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\cos ^{-1}\left\{\cos 2 \cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{2}-1)\right\}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \cos ^{-1}[\cos \{2 & \left.\left.\cdot \cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{2}-1)\right\}\right] \\ & =\cos ^{-1}[\cos \{2 \times 67.5\}] \\ & =\cos ^{-1}\left[\cos 135^{\circ}\right] \\ & =135^{\circ} \\ & =\frac{3 \pi}{4}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\sum_{m=1}^n \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 m}{m^4+m^2+2}\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned}
& \sum_{m=1}^n \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 m}{m^4+m^2+2}\right) \\
&= \sum_{m=1}^n \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{2 m}{1+\left(m^2+m+1\right)\left(m^2-m+1\right)}\right] \\
&= \sum_{m=1}^n \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\left(m^2+m+1\right)-\left(m^2-m+1\right)}{1+\left(m^2+m+1\right)\left(m^2-m+1\right)}\right] \\
&= \sum_{m=1}^n\left\{\tan ^{-1}\left[m^2+m+1\right]-\tan ^{-1}\left[m^2-m+1\right]\right\} \\
&= \tan ^{-1} 3-\tan ^{-1} 1+\tan ^{-1} 7-\tan ^{-1} 3 \\
&+\left(\tan ^{-1} 13-\tan ^{-1} 7\right)+\ldots+\tan ^{-1}\left(n^2+n+1\right) \\
&-\tan ^{-1}\left(n^2-n+1\right)
\end{aligned}$
$=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{n^2+n+1-1}{1+\left(n^2+n+1\right) \cdot 1}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{n^2+n}{2+n^2+n}\right)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\tan \left\{\frac{1}{2} \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}\right)\right\}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}\right)=\alpha$. then $\cos \alpha=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{3}$, where $0 \lt \alpha \lt \frac{\pi}{2}$.
Now, $\tan \frac{\alpha}{2}=\sqrt{\frac{1-\cos \alpha}{1+\cos \alpha}}=\sqrt{\frac{1-\sqrt{5} / 3}{1+\sqrt{5} / 3}}$
$=\sqrt{\frac{3-\sqrt{5}}{3+\sqrt{5}}}=\sqrt{\frac{(3-\sqrt{5})^2}{9-5}}=\frac{1}{2}(3-\sqrt{5})$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The interval for which $2 \tan ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} \frac{2 x}{1+x^2}$ is independent of $x$ is
questions.solution:
We know that;
if $|x| \leq 1$, then $2 \tan ^{-1} x=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)$
and if $|x|\gt1$, then $\pi-2 \tan ^{-1} x=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)$
thus, if $|x|\gt1$.
$\begin{aligned} 2 \tan ^{-1} x+\sin ^{-1} \frac{2 x}{1+x^2}=2 \tan ^{-1} x+\pi \\ -2 \tan ^{-1} x=\pi\end{aligned}$
which is independent of $x$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
The value of
$\sin ^{-1}\left[\operatorname { c o s } \left(\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}}\right.\right.\left.\left.+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{12}}{4}+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right]$ is
questions.solution:
We have, 
$\sin ^{-1}\left[\cos \left(\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{\left(\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}\right)}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{12}}{4}+\sec ^{-1} \sqrt{2}\right)\right]$
$=\sin ^{-1}\left[\cos \left(\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)+\cos ^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right]$
$\left[\because\left(\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4}\right)^{1 / 2}=\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right]$
$=\sin ^{-1}\left[\cos \left(15^{\circ}+30^{\circ}+45^{\circ}\right)\right]$
$=\sin ^{-1}\left[\cos 90^{\circ}\right] \quad\left(\because \sin 15^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =\sin ^{-1} 0 \\ & =0\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin x}+\sqrt{1-\sin x}}{\sqrt{1+\sin x}-\sqrt{1-\sin x}}\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $l=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin x}+\sqrt{1-\sin x}}{\sqrt{1+\sin x}-\sqrt{1-\sin x}}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =\cot ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{\left(\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}+\sqrt{\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}-\sin \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}}{\sqrt{\left(\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}-\sqrt{\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}-\sin \frac{x}{2}\right)^2}}\right] \\ & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}-\sin \frac{x}{2}}{\left.\sin \frac{x}{2}+\cos \frac{x}{2}-\cos \frac{x}{2}+\sin \frac{x}{2}\right)}\right. \\ & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 \cos \frac{x}{2}}{2 \sin \frac{x}{2}}\right)=\cot ^{-1}\left(\cot \frac{x}{2}\right)=\frac{x}{2}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $4 \sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x=\pi$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $4 \sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x=\pi$
$\Rightarrow  4 \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{\pi}{2}-\sin ^{-1} x=\pi$
$\Rightarrow  3 \sin ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}$
$\Rightarrow  x=\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right)=\frac{1}{2}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\begin{aligned} & \text { If } \tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi \text {, then } \\ & x+y+z \text { is }\end{aligned}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  } \tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} y+\tan ^{-1} z=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1}\left\{\frac{x+y+z-x y z}{1-x y-y z-z x}\right\}=\pi \\ & \Rightarrow x+y+z-x y z=0 \Rightarrow x+y+z=x y z\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\tan \left(\sec ^{-1} x\right)=\sin \left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right)$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{array}{rlrl} & & \tan \left(\sec ^{-1} x\right) & =\sin \left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right) \\ \Rightarrow & & \sqrt{\sec ^2\left(\sec ^{-1} x\right)-1}=\sqrt{1-\cos ^2\left(\cos ^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{5}}\right)} \\ \Rightarrow & & \sqrt{x^2-1}=\sqrt{1-\frac{1}{5}} \\ \Rightarrow & & x^2-1=\frac{4}{5}\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & x^2=\frac{9}{5} \\ \Rightarrow & x= \pm \frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\sin ^{-1} a+\sin ^{-1} b+\sin ^{-1} c=\pi$, then the value of $a \cdot \sqrt{\left(1-a^2\right)}+b \cdot \sqrt{\left(1-b^2\right)}+c \cdot \sqrt{\left(1-c^2\right)}$ will be
questions.solution:
Let $\sin ^{-1} a=A$
$\begin{aligned}
\sin ^{-1} b & =B \\
\sin ^{-1} C & =C
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad \sin A=a, \quad \sin B=b, \quad \sin C=c$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { and } A+B+C=\pi \text {, then } \\
& \begin{aligned}
\sin 2 A+\sin 2 B+ & \sin 2 C \\
& =4 \sin A \sin B \sin C...(i)
\end{aligned}
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{gathered}
\Rightarrow \quad \sin A \cos A+\sin B \cos B+\sin C \cos C \\
=2 \sin A \sin B \sin C \\
\Rightarrow \quad \sin A \sqrt{1-\sin ^2 A}+\sin B \sqrt{1-\sin ^2 B} \\
\quad+\sin C \sqrt{1-\sin ^2 C}=2 \sin A \sin B \sin C \ldots (ii)\\
\Rightarrow \quad a \sqrt{1-a^2}+b \sqrt{1-b^2}+c \sqrt{1-c^2}=2 a b c \\
\quad \text { while } \sin ^{-1} a+\sin ^{-1} b+\sin ^{-1} c=\pi
\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \frac{7 \pi}{6}\right)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \frac{7 \pi}{6}\right)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(2 \pi-\frac{5 \pi}{6}\right)\right) \\ =\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{6}\right)\right)=\frac{5 \pi}{6}\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\operatorname{cosec} \tan ^{-1} x-\tan \cot ^{-1} x\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
The given expression
$\begin{aligned}
& =2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\operatorname{cosec} \tan ^{-1} x-\tan \cot ^{-1} x\right] \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\operatorname{cosec}\left\{\operatorname{cosec}^{-1} \frac{\sqrt{1+x^2}}{x}\right\}\right.  \left.-\tan \left\{\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)\right\}\right] \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+x^2}}{x}-\frac{1}{x}\right]=2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sqrt{1+x^2}-1}{x}\right] \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{\sec \theta-1}{\tan \theta}\right] {[putting \quad x=\tan \theta]} \\
= & \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{1-\cos \theta}{\sin \theta}\right] \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1}\left[\frac{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2}}{2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2} \cdot \cos \frac{\theta}{2}}\right] \\
= & 2 \tan ^{-1} \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=2 \cdot \frac{\theta}{2}=\theta=\tan ^{-1} x
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The number of solutions of the equation $2 \sin ^{-1} \sqrt{x^2-x+1}+\cos ^{-1} \sqrt{x^2-x}=\frac{3 \pi}{2}$ is
questions.solution:
Since, $\sin ^{-1} \sqrt{x}, \cos ^{-1} \sqrt{x}$ are defined for $x \leq 1$ and $x \geq 0$.
$\therefore \quad 0 \leq \sqrt{x^2-x+1} \leq 1$
and $\quad 0 \leq \sqrt{x^2-x} \leq 1$
$\Rightarrow \quad-1 \leq x^2-x \leq 0$
and $\quad 0 \leq x^2-x \leq 0$
$\begin{array}{lr}\Rightarrow & x^2-x=0 \\ \Rightarrow & x=0,1\end{array}$
Hence, number of solutions are two.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
If $f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\sin [x]}{[x]} & \text { for }[x] \neq 0 \\ 0 & \text { for }[x]=0\end{cases}$
where, $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to $x$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)$ is equal to
questions.solution: Hence, limit does not exist.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sin \sqrt{t} d t}{x^3}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sin \sqrt{t} d t}{x^3} \quad\left(\right.$ form $\left.\frac{0}{0}\right)$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin \sqrt{x^2} \times 2 x}{3 x^2}$
(using L' Hospital's rule)
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2}{3} \frac{\sin x}{x}=\frac{2}{3} \times 1=\frac{2}{3}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $F: R \rightarrow R$ is a differentiable function and $f(3)=6$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow 3} \int_6^{f(x)} \frac{2 t d t}{(x-2)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $I=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3} \int_6^{f(x)} \frac{2 t}{x-2} d t\left[\frac{0}{0}\right.$ form, as $\left.f(3)=6\right]$
On applying the L' Hospital rule, we get
$I=\lim _{x \rightarrow 3} \int_6^{f(x)} \frac{2 f(x) f^{\prime}(x)}{1}$ $(\because f(3)=6)$
So, $I=3 f(3) \cdot f^{\prime}(3)=18 f^{\prime}(3)$
$\therefore \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 3} \int_6^{f(x)} \frac{2 t d t}{x-2}=18 f^{\prime}(3)$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
Let $f(x)$ be differentiable on the interval $(0, \infty)$ such that $f(1)=1$ and
$\lim _{t \rightarrow x} \frac{t^2 f(x)-x^2 f(t)}{t-x}=1$ for each $x\gt0$
Then, $f(x)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $\lim _{t \rightarrow x} \frac{t^2 f(x)-x^2 f(t)}{t-x}=1$
Using L' Hospital's rule, we get
$\lim _{t \rightarrow x} \frac{t^2 f^{\prime}(x)-2 x f(t)}{-1}=1$
$\Rightarrow \quad x^2 f^{\prime}(x)-2 x f(x)+1=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x^2 f^{\prime}(x)-2 x f(x)}{\left(x^2\right)^2}+\frac{1}{x^4}=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{f(x)}{x^2}\right)=-\frac{1}{x^4}$
On integrating both sides, we get
$\frac{f(x)}{x^2}=+\frac{1}{3 x^3}+c \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{3 x}+c x^2$
Also, $\quad f(1)=1 \Rightarrow 1=\frac{1}{3 \times 1}+c(1)^2$
$\Rightarrow \quad 1=\frac{1}{3}+c \Rightarrow \frac{2}{3}=c$
$\therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{1}{3 x}+\frac{2 x^2}{3}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sec ^2 t d t}{x \sin x}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $I^{\prime}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \sec ^2 t d t}{x \sin x} \quad\left[\frac{0}{0}\right.$ form $]$
Using L'Hospital's rule, $=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sec ^2 x^2 \cdot 2 x}{x \cos x+\sin x} \quad\left[\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right]$
Again using L'Hospital's rule, $\begin{aligned} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} & \frac{2 x \cdot 2 \sec ^2 x^2 \cdot \tan x^2 \cdot 2 x+2 \sec ^2 x^2}{-x \sin x+\cos x+\cos x} \\ & =\frac{0+2 \sec ^2 0}{0+2 \cos 0}=1 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\tan \theta}{\theta}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{gathered}\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\tan \theta}{\theta}. \text { Applying L'Hospital's rule, } \\ =\lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sec ^2 \theta}{1}=1\end{gathered}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\left[\frac{\sin x}{x}\right]+\left[\frac{2 \sin x}{x}\right]+\ldots+\left[\frac{10 \sin x}{x}\right]$ (where [.] is the largest integer). then, find the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)$.
questions.solution:
As we know,
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} \simeq 1$
but it is actually less than 1 .
So, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{\sin x}{x}\right]=0=1^2-1$
Similarly, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[2 \frac{\sin 2 x}{x}\right]=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[4 \frac{\sin 2 x}{2 x}\right]$
$=3=2^2-1$
Hence, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[n \frac{\sin n x}{x}\right]=n^2-1$
Therefore,
$\begin{aligned}
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)=\left(1^2-1\right)+\left(2^2-1\right)+\ldots+\left(10^2-(1)\right. \\
& =\left(1^2+2^2+\ldots+10^2\right)-(1+1+\ldots+1 \text { times }) \\
& =\frac{10 \times 11 \times 21}{6}-10 \\
& =385-10=375
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $f(x)=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x-x^3}{1-3 x^2}\right)$ and $g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow a} \frac{f(x)-f(a)}{g(x)-g(a)}, 0 \lt a \lt \frac{1}{2}$, i
questions.solution:
Given that, $f(x)=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 x-x^3}{1-3 x^2}\right)$ and $g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)$ Put $x=\tan \theta$ in $f(x)$, we get $\begin{aligned} f(x) & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \tan \theta-\tan ^3 \theta}{1-3 \tan ^2 \theta}\right) \\ \Rightarrow \quad f(x) & =\frac{\pi}{2}-3 \tan ^{-1} x \end{aligned}$
Again put $x=\tan \theta$ in $g(x)$, we get $\begin{aligned} & \quad g(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-\tan ^2 \theta}{1+\tan ^2 \theta}\right) \\ & g(x)=2 \tan ^{-1} x \\ & \therefore \lim _{x \rightarrow a} \frac{f(x)-f(a)}{g(x)-g(a)} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow a} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-3 \tan ^{-1} x\right)-\frac{\pi}{2}+3 \tan ^{-1} a}{2 \tan ^{-1} x-2 \tan ^{-1} a} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow a} \frac{-3\left(\tan ^{-1} a-\tan ^{-1} x\right)}{2\left(\tan ^{-1} a-\tan ^{-1} x\right)}=-\frac{3}{2} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow-2} \frac{\sin ^{-1}(x+2)}{x^2+2 x}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { We have, } y=\lim _{x \rightarrow-2} \frac{\sin ^{-1}(x+2)}{x^2+2 x} \\ & \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad y=\lim _{x \rightarrow-2} \frac{\sin ^{-1}(x+2)}{(x+2)} \times \frac{1}{x} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=\lim _{x \rightarrow-2}(1) \times \frac{1}{x} \quad\left[\because \lim _{x \rightarrow \theta} \frac{\sin \theta}{\theta}=1\right] \\ &=-\frac{1}{2}\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Let $U_n=2+2^3+2^5+\ldots .+2^{2 n+1}$ and $V_n=1+4+4^2+\ldots .+4^{n-1}$. Then, $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{U_n}{V_n}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given, $U_n=2+2^3+2^5+2^7+\ldots+2^{2 n+1}$
and $V_n=1+4+4^2+\ldots+4^{n-1}$
$\therefore \quad U_n=\frac{l r-a}{r-1}=\frac{2^{2 n+1} \cdot 4-2}{4-1}=\frac{2^{2 n} \cdot 8-2}{3}$
and $\quad ' V_n=\frac{l r-a}{r-1}=\frac{4^{n-1} \cdot 4-1}{4-1}=\frac{2^{2 n}-1}{3}$
Now, $\quad \frac{U_n}{V_n}=\frac{2^{2 n} \cdot 8-2}{2^{2 n}-1}=\frac{8-\frac{2}{2^{2 n}}}{1-\frac{1}{2^{2 n}}}$
$\therefore \quad \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{U_n}{V_n}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{8-\frac{2}{2^{2 n}}}{1-\frac{1}{2^{2 n}}}=\frac{8-0}{1-0}=8$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
If $f^{\prime \prime}(0)=k$, then
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 f(x)-3 f(2 x)+f(4 x)}{x^2}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \because f^{\prime \prime}(0) & =k \text { and } \\ & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 f(x)-3 f(2 x)+f(4 x)}{x^2}\end{aligned}$
Using L'Hospital's rule, $=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 f^{\prime}(x)-6 f^{\prime}(2 x)+4 f^{\prime}(4 x)}{2 x}$
Again, using L'Hospital's rule, $\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 f^{\prime \prime}(x)-12 f^{\prime \prime}(2 x)+16 f^{\prime \prime}(4 x)}{2} \\ & =\frac{1}{2}\left[2 f^{\prime \prime}(0)-12 f^{\prime \prime}(0)+16 f^{\prime \prime}(0)\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{2}[2 k-12 k+16 k]=3 k \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{(2+x) \sin (2+x)-2 \sin 2}{x}\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} & \frac{(2+x) \sin (2+x)-2 \sin 2}{x}\left(\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right) \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin (2+x)+(2+x) \cos (2+x)}{1} \\ & =\sin 2+2 \cos 2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{4 n^2-1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4 n^2-2^2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3 n^2}}\right)$
equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{4 n^2-1}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4 n^2-2^2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{3 n^2}}\right) \\ = & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)^2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-\left(\frac{2}{n}\right)^2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-\left(\frac{n}{n}\right)^2}}\right] \\ = & \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} h\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-h^2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-(2 h)^2}}+\ldots+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4-(n h)^2}}\right] \\ = & \int_0^1 \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4-x^2}}=\left[\sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right]_0^1 \\ = & \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\sin ^{-1}(0)=\frac{\pi}{6}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin 3 x-\sin x}{\sin x}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin 3 x-\sin x}{\sin x} \\ &=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \cos 2 x \sin x}{\sin x}=2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1-\cos m x}{1-\cos n x}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1-\cos m x}{1-\cos n x}\right) \\ = & \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \sin ^2(m x / 2)}{(m x / 2)^2} \cdot \frac{(n x / 2)^2}{2 \sin ^2(n x / 2)} \cdot \frac{m^2}{n^2}=\frac{m^2}{n^2}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\sqrt[3]{n^2-n^3}+n\right]$ is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\sqrt[3]{n^2-n^3}+n\right]=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n\left[\left(-1+\frac{1}{n}\right)^{1 / 3}+1^{1 / 3}\right]$
$=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n \cdot \frac{\left(\frac{1}{n}-1\right)+1}{\left(\frac{1}{n}-1\right)^{2 / 3}+1-\left(\frac{1}{n}-1\right)^{1 / 3}}$  $\left[\because a+b=\frac{a^3+b^3}{a^2-a b+b^2}\right]$ 
$=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{\left(\frac{1}{n}-1\right)^{2 / 3}+1-\left(\frac{1}{n}-1\right)^{1 / 3}}$
$=\frac{1}{1+1+1}=\frac{1}{3}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow \pi / 6} \frac{2 \sin ^2 x+\sin x-1}{2 \sin ^2 x-3 \sin x+1}$ is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{2 \sin ^2 x+\sin x-1}{2 \sin ^2 x-3 \sin x+1}$
$=\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{4 \sin x \cos x+\cos x}{4 \sin x \cos x-3 \cos x}$   [by L' Hospital's rule]
$\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{6}} \frac{\cos x(4 \sin x+1)}{\cos x(4 \sin x-3)} \\ & =\frac{4 \sin \frac{\pi}{6}+1}{4 \sin \frac{\pi}{6}-3}=-3\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow-1}\left(\frac{x^4+x^2+x+1}{x^2-x+1}\right)^{\frac{1-\cos (x+1)}{(x+1)^2}}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
We observe that
$\frac{x^4+x^2+x+1}{x^2-x+1} \rightarrow \frac{2}{3}$ as $x \rightarrow-1$
But $\quad \frac{1-\cos (x+1)}{(x+1)^2} \rightarrow \frac{0}{0}$ as $x \rightarrow-1$
$\therefore \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow-1}\left(\frac{x^4+x^2+x+1}{x^2-x+1}\right)^{\frac{1-\cos (x+1)}{(x+1)^2}}$
$=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right) x^{\lim _{x \rightarrow-1} \frac{1-\cos (x+1)}{(x+1)^2}}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{\lim _{x \rightarrow-1} \frac{2 \sin ^2\left(\frac{x+1}{2}\right)}{4\left(\frac{x+1}{2}\right)^2}}=\left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{1 / 2}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left\{\frac{a_1^{1 / x}+a_2^{1 / x}+\ldots+a_n^{1 / x}}{n}\right\}^{n x}$, is
questions.solution:
Let $x=\frac{1}{y}$. Then,
$\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left\{\frac{a_1^{1 / x}+a_2^{1 / x}+\ldots+a_n^{1 / x}}{n}\right\}^{n x} \\ &=\lim _{y \rightarrow 0}\left\{\frac{a_1^y+a_2^y+\ldots+a_n^y}{n}\right\}^{n / y} \\ &=\lim _{y \rightarrow 0}\left\{1+\frac{a_1^y+a_2^y+\ldots+a_n^y-n}{n}\right\}^{n / y} \\ &=e^{y \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{a_1^y-1}{y}+\frac{a_2^y-1}{y}+\ldots+\frac{a_n^y-1}{y}\right\} \\ &=e^{\log a_1+\log a_2+\ldots+\log a_n} \\ &=e^{\log \left(a_1 a_2 \ldots a_n\right)} \\ &=a_1 a_2 a_3 \ldots a_n\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $f(x)=\frac{\sin \left(e^{x-2}-1\right)}{\log (x-1)}$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} f(x)$ is given by
questions.solution:
We have,
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} f(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{\sin \left(e^{x-2}-1\right)}{\log (x-1)}$
$\begin{aligned} &= \lim _{x \rightarrow 2}\left\{\frac{\sin \left(e^{x-2}-1\right)}{e^{x-2}-1} \cdot \cdot \frac{e^{x-2}-1}{x-2} \frac{x-2}{\log [1+(x-2)]}\right\}\end{aligned}$
$=1 \times 1 \times 1=1$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{\sin x}-1}{x}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{\sin x}-1}{x} & \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left[\frac{\left(e^{\sin x}-1\right)}{\sin x} \times \frac{\sin x}{x}\right] \\ & =\lim _{\sin x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{\sin x}-1}{\sin x} \times \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} \\ & =1 \times 1=1\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(1-\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)(1-\sin x)}{\left(1+\tan \frac{x}{2}\right)(\pi-2 x)^3}$
is equal to
questions.solution:
Put $x=\frac{\pi}{2}-h$ as $x \rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}, h \rightarrow 0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \text { Given, } \operatorname{limit}=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{1-\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{h}{2}\right)(1-\cos h)}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{h}{2}\right)(2 h)^3} \\
& \quad=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \tan \left(\frac{h}{2}\right) \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \cdot \sin ^2\left(\frac{h}{2}\right)}{8 h^3} \\
& \quad=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{\tan \frac{h}{2}}{2 \times \frac{h}{2}}\right) \times \lim _{h \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{\sin \frac{h}{2}}{\frac{h}{2}}\right)^2 \times \frac{1}{4} \\
& \\
& \quad=\frac{1}{32}
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $f(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{8^x+2^x+4^x}{3}\right)^{2 / x}$, then $f(x)^{\prime}$ equals to
questions.solution:
$y=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{8^x+2^x+4^x}{3}\right)^{2 / x}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \log y=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2}{x} \log \left(\frac{8^x+2^x+4^x}{3}\right) \\ & =2 \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{x}\left[\log \left(8^x+2^x+4^x\right)-\log 3\right]\end{aligned}$
$=2 \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\left[\log \left(8^x+2^x+4^x\right)-\log 3\right]}{x} \quad\left[\frac{0}{0}\right.$ form $]$
On applying L' Hospital rule, we get
$\begin{aligned} & =2 \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\left[8^x \log 8+2^x \log 2+4^x \log 4\right]}{8^x+2^x+4^x} \\ & =2\left[\frac{\log 8+\log 2+\log 4}{3}\right]\end{aligned}$
$=\frac{2}{3}[3 \log 2+\log 2+2 \log 2] \quad\left[\log (m)^n=n \log m\right]$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{2}{3} \times 6 \log 2 \\ & \log y=4 \log 2=\log 16 \Rightarrow y=16\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of the $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{a^x+b^x+c^x}{3}\right)^{2 / x}$, $(a, b, c\gt0)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } y=\lim _{x \rightarrow 10}\left(\frac{a^x+b^x+c^x}{3}\right)^{2 / x} \\ & \begin{array}{l}\Rightarrow \log y=\lim _{x \rightarrow 10} \frac{2}{x} \log \left(\frac{a^x+b^x+c^x}{3}\right) \\ \quad=\lim _{x \rightarrow 10} \frac{\log \left(a^x+b^x+c^x\right)-\log 3}{x} \\ \Rightarrow \log y=\log (a b c)^{2 / 3} \quad \text { (by L-Hospital's rule) } \\ \Rightarrow \quad y=(a b c)^{2 / 3}\end{array}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{x^2+2^{2-x}-5}{2^{-x / 2}-2^{1-x}}$ is equal to _____
questions.solution:
We have, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{2^x+2^{2-x}-5}{2^{-x / 2}-2^{1-x}} \quad\left[\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right]$
So, apply L' Hospital's rule $=\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{\frac{d}{d x}\left(2^x+2^{2-x}-5\right)}{\frac{d}{d x}\left(2^{-x / 2}-2^{1-x}\right)}$ $\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{2^x \ln 2-2^{2-x} \ln 2}{2^{1-x} \ln 2-\frac{2^{-\frac{x}{2}} \ln 2}{2}} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{2^{x+1}-2^{3-x}}{2^{2-x}-2^{-\frac{x}{2}}} \\ & =\frac{2^3-2^1}{2^0-2^{-1}}=\frac{8-2}{2-\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{12}{2-1}=12\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin 3 x-\sin x}{\sin x}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} & \frac{\sin 3 x-\sin x}{\sin x} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \cos 2 x \sin x}{\sin x}=2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(1^{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}+2^{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}+\ldots+n^{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}\right)^{\sin ^2 x}$
is equal to
questions.solution:
Let $y=\operatorname{cosec}^2 x$,
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Required limit }=\lim _{y \rightarrow \infty}\left(y^y+2^y+\ldots n^y\right)^{1 / y} \\
& =\lim _{y \rightarrow \infty}\left(n^y\right)^{1 / y} \quad(0 / \infty \text { form }) \\
& \qquad\left[\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)^y+\left(\frac{2}{n}\right)^y+\ldots+\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right)^y+1\right]^{1 / y} \\
& =\lim _{y \rightarrow \infty} n\left[\left(\frac{1}{n}\right)^y+\left(\frac{2}{n}\right)^y+\ldots+\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right)^y+1\right]^{1 / y} \\
& =n \cdot 1^0=n \cdot 1=n
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin (\sin x)-\sin x}{a x^3+b x^5+c}=\frac{-1}{12}$, then
questions.solution:
Given, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin (\sin x)-\sin x}{a x^3+b x^5+c}=-\frac{1}{12}$    ....(i)
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\sin \sin 0-\sin 0}{a(0)^3+b(0)^5+c}=-\frac{1}{12}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{0}{c}=-\frac{1}{12} \Rightarrow c=0$
Applying L' Hospital's rule in Eq.(i),we get
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\cos (\sin x) \cos x-\cos x}{3 a x^2+5 b x^4+0}=-\frac{1}{12}$
$\Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\cos x(\cos (\sin x)-1)}{x^2(3 a+5 b x)}=-\frac{1}{12}$
$\Rightarrow \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \cos x \frac{2 \sin ^2\left(\frac{\sin x}{2}\right)}{x^2(3 a+5 b x)}=-\frac{1}{12}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\cos 0}{3 a+5 b \times 0} \times \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \sin ^2\left(\frac{\sin x}{2}\right)}{4\left(\frac{\sin x}{2}\right)^2 \times\left(\frac{x}{\sin x}\right)^2}=-\frac{1}{12}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{3 a+0} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{1}=-\frac{1}{12}\left(\because \lim _{\theta \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin \theta}{\theta}=1\right)$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{6 a}=-\frac{1}{12} \Rightarrow 6 a=-12$
$\therefore \quad a=-2$
Hence, $a=-2, b \in R$ and $c=0$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{\left[\sum_{K=1}^{100}-x^K\right]-100}{x-1}$ is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{\left[\sum_{K=1}^{100} x^K\right]-100}{(x-1)}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{\left(x+x^2+x^3+\ldots+x^{100}\right)-100}{(x-1)} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{(x-1)+\left(x^2-1\right)+\left(x^3-1\right)+\ldots+\left(x^{100}-1\right)}{(x-1)}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left\{\left(\frac{x-1}{x-1}\right)+\left(\frac{x^2-1}{x-1}\right)+\left(\frac{x^3-1}{x-1}\right)\right. & \\ & \left.+\ldots+\left(\frac{x^{100}-1}{x-1}\right)\right\}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left(\frac{x-1}{x-1}\right)+\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left(\frac{x^2-1}{x-1}\right) & +\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left(\frac{x^3-1}{x-1}\right) \\ & +\ldots+\lim _{x \rightarrow 1}\left(\frac{x^{100}-1}{x-1}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =1+2+3+\ldots+100 \\ & =\Sigma 100=\frac{100 \times(100+1)}{2}\end{aligned}$
$=50 \times 101=5050 \quad\left\{\because \Sigma n=\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right\}$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $y=\frac{x}{x+\frac{\sqrt[3]{x}}{x+\sqrt[3]{x} \ldots \text { infinity }}}$
$=\frac{x}{x+\frac{1}{x^{2 / 3}} \cdot \frac{x}{x+\sqrt[3]{x} \ldots \infty}}=\frac{x}{x+\frac{y}{x^{2 / 3}}}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow y=\frac{x^{5 / 3}}{x^{5 / 3}+y} \\ & \Rightarrow y^2+\left(x^{5 / 3}\right) \cdot y-x^{5 / 3}=0 \\ & \therefore \quad y=\frac{-x^{5 / 3} \pm \sqrt{x^{10 / 3}+4 x^{5 / 3}}}{2}\end{aligned}$
$=\frac{-x^{5 / 3}+\sqrt{x^{10 / 3}+4 x^{5 / 3}}}{2}$ $(\because y\gt0)$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{4 x^{5 / 3}}{2\left(\sqrt{x^{10 / 3}+4 x^{5 / 3}}+x^{5 / 3}\right)}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{5 / 3}}\right)+1}} \\ & \begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} y=\frac{2}{\sqrt{1+0}+1} \\ & \quad=\frac{2}{2}=1 .\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q:
If $f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\sin [x]}{[x]}, & {[x] \neq 0} \\ 0, & {[x]=0}\end{cases}$
where $[x]$ denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to $x$, then $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)$ equals
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { As, } f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\sin [x]}{[x]}, & {[x] \neq 0} \\
0, & {[x]=0}\end{cases} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{\sin [x]}{[x]}, & x \in R-[0,1) \\
0, & 0 \leq x \lt 1\end{cases} \\
& \therefore \quad \text { RHL at } x=0 \\
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} f(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin [0+h]}{[0+h]}=0 \\
& \text { LHL at } x=0 \\
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} f(x)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin [0-h]}{[0-h]} \\
& =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin (-1)}{-1}=\sin 1
\end{aligned}$
Since, RHL $\neq$ LHL
$\therefore$ Limit does not exist.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \cos ^2 t d t}{x \sin x}$ is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\int_0^{x^2} \cos ^2 t d t}{x \sin x}$
Using L Hospital's rule, we get $\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\cos ^2\left(x^2\right) \cdot 2 x-0}{x \cos x+\sin x} \\ & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 \cos ^2\left(x^2\right)}{\cos x+\frac{\sin x}{x}} \\ & =\frac{2}{2}=1 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: If $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(1+\frac{a}{x}+\frac{b}{x^2}\right)^{2 x}=e^2$, then
questions.solution:
Since, $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(1+\frac{a}{x}+\frac{b}{x^2}\right)^{2 x}=e^2$
$\Rightarrow \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(1+\frac{a x+b}{x^2}\right)^{2 x}-e^2$..(i)
$\Rightarrow \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a x+b}{x^2}$ must be equal to zero.
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{a x+b}{x^2}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{a}{x}+\frac{b}{x^2}\right)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a \text { and } b \neq 0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ From Eq. (i)
$\begin{aligned} & e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2(a x+b)}{x}}=e^2 \\ \Rightarrow \quad & e^{2 a}=e^2 \\ \Rightarrow \quad & a=1 \text { and } b \in R\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{1-\cos 2 x}}{\sqrt{2} \cdot x}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{gathered}\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{1-\cos 2 x}}{\sqrt{2} \cdot x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{1-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 x\right)}}{\sqrt{2} \cdot x} \\ \quad=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{2 \sin ^2 x}}{\sqrt{2} \cdot x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{|\sin x|}{x}\end{gathered}$
Let $f(x)=\frac{|\sin x|}{x}$
Then,
$f(0+0)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{|\sin (0+h)|}{h}=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin h}{h}=1$
and
$f(0-0)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{|\sin (0-h)|}{-h}=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin h}{-h}=-1$
$\because  f(0+0) \neq f(0-0)$
$\therefore$ The limit does not exist.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1}{n+1}+\frac{1}{n+2}+\ldots+\frac{1}{6 n}\right]$
